raw_question_text:
The "charmed" particles observed in $e^{+} e^{-}$storage rings have not yet been seen in hadron-hadron interactions. One possible means for detecting such particles is the observation of muons resulting from their leptonic decays. For example, consider a charmed particle $c$ with decay mode 
$$ c \rightarrow \mu 
u $$ Unfortunately, the experimental situation is complicated by the presence of muons from $\pi$ decays. Consider an experiment at Fermilab in which 400 GeV protons strike a thick iron target (beam dump) as depicted in Fig. 4.13. Some of the muons entering the detector will be from $\pi$ decays and some from $c$ decays (ignore other processes). Calculate the ratio of muons from $c$ decays to those from $\pi$ decays under the following assumptions: the pions that have suffered interaction in the dump completely disappear from the beam, the energy spectra of both $\pi$ and $c$ are flat from minimum up to the maximum possible energy, the mass of the $c$ is $2 \mathrm{GeV} / c^{2}$ and its lifetime is $\ll 10^{-10} \mathrm{sec}$, one can ignore muon energy loss in the iron, one can ignore any complications due to the geometry of the muon detector, the $p-p$ inelastic cross section is 30 mb , and the mean charged pion multiplicity in inelastic interactions is 8 . Be specific. State any additional assumptions. Give a numerical value for the ratio at $E_{\mu}=100 \mathrm{GeV}$ assuming the total production cross section for $c$ to be $10 \mu b$ per Fe nucleus and that it decays to $\mu 
u 10 \%$ of the time.
normalized_question_text: The "charmed" particles observed in $e^{+} e^{-}$ storage rings have not yet been seen in hadron-hadron interactions. One possible means for detecting such particles is the observation of muons resulting from their leptonic decays. For example, consider a charmed particle $c$ with decay mode $$c \rightarrow \mu \nu$$. Unfortunately, the experimental situation is complicated by the presence of muons from $\pi$ decays. Consider an experiment at Fermilab in which 400 GeV protons strike a thick iron target (beam dump) as depicted in Fig. 4.13. Some of the muons entering the detector will be from $\pi$ decays and some from $c$ decays (ignore other processes). Calculate the ratio of muons from $c$ decays to those from $\pi$ decays under the following assumptions: the pions that have suffered interaction in the dump completely disappear from the beam; the energy spectra of both $\pi$ and $c$ are flat from minimum up to the maximum possible energy; the mass of the $c$ is $2 \, \mathrm{GeV}/c^{2}$ and its lifetime is $\ll 10^{-10} \, \mathrm{sec}$; one can ignore muon energy loss in the iron; one can ignore any complications due to the geometry of the muon detector; the $p$-$p$ inelastic cross section is 30 mb; and the mean charged pion multiplicity in inelastic interactions is 8. Be specific. State any additional assumptions. Give a numerical value for the ratio at $E_{\mu} = 100 \, \mathrm{GeV}$ assuming the total production cross section for $c$ to be $10 \, \mu\mathrm{b}$ per Fe nucleus and that it decays to $\mu \nu$ 10% of the time.
reasoning_chain:
In addition to the assumptions listed in the question, we also assume the charge independence of nucleon interactions so that $\sigma_{p p}=\sigma_{p n}$ and the mean charged-pion multiplicities are the same for $p p$ and $p n$ collisions.

For ${ }^{56} \mathrm{Fe}$, the number densities of protons and neutrons are the same, being
$$
N_{p}=N_{n}=\frac{28}{56} \times 7.8 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}=2.35 \times 10^{24} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}
$$

Denote the flux of protons in the incident beam by $\phi(x)$, where $x$ is the target thickness from the surface of incidence. As
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{d \phi}{d x} & =-\left(\sigma_{p p} N_{p}+\sigma_{p n} N_{n}\right) \phi=-2 \sigma_{p p} N_{p} \phi 
\phi & =\phi_{0} e^{-2 \sigma_{p p} N_{p} x}
\end{aligned}
$$

If the target is sufficiently thick, say $x=10 \mathrm{~m}=10^{3} \mathrm{~cm}$, then
$$
\phi=\phi_{0} \exp \left(-2 \times 30 \times 10^{-27} \times 2.35 \times 10^{24} \times 10^{3}\right)=5.8 \times 10^{-62} \phi_{0},
$$
at the exit surface, showing that the beam of protons is completely dissipated in the target. This will be assumed in the following.

Consider first the $c$ quarks produced in $p-\mathrm{Fe}$ interactions in the target. From the given data $\sigma_{p \mathrm{Fe}}(c)=10 \mu b, \sigma_{p p}=30 \mathrm{mb}$, we find the number of $c$ quarks so produced as
$$
\begin{aligned}
N_{c} & =\int N_{\mathrm{Fe}} \sigma(c) d \phi \approx N_{\mathrm{Fe}} \sigma(c) \phi_{0} \int_{0}^{\infty} e^{-2 \sigma_{p p} N_{p} x} d x 
& =\frac{N_{\mathrm{Fe}}}{2 N_{p p}} \frac{\sigma(c)}{\sigma_{p p}} \phi_{0}=\frac{1}{56} \times \frac{10^{-5}}{30 \times 10^{-3}} \phi_{0}=5.95 \times 10^{-6} \phi_{0}
\end{aligned}
$$

As the $c$ quarks have lifetime $\ll 10^{-10}$ s, all those produced in $p-\mathrm{Fe}$ interactions will decay in the target, giving rise to muons $10 \%$ of the times. Thus
$$
N_{\mu c}=0.1 N_{c}=5.95 \times 10^{-7} \phi_{0} .
$$

Next consider the muons arising from the decay of charged pions produced in $p$-nucleon interactions. After emission the pions may interact
with the nucleons of the target and disappear from the beam, as assumed, or decay in flight giving rise to muons. For the former case we assume $\sigma_{\pi p}=\sigma_{\pi n} \approx \frac{2}{3} \sigma_{p p}=20 \mathrm{mb}$ at high energies. For the latter case the lifetime of the charged pions in the laboratory is $\gamma_{\pi} / \lambda$, where $\lambda$ is the decay constant and $\gamma_{\pi}=\left(1-\beta_{\pi}^{2}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2}}, \beta_{\pi} c$ being the mean velocity of the pions. Then the change of $N_{\pi}$ per unit interval of $x$ is
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{d N_{\pi}}{d x} & =8\left(\sigma_{p p} N_{p}+\sigma_{p n} N_{n}\right) \phi(x)-\left(\frac{\lambda}{\gamma_{\pi} \beta_{\pi} c}+\sigma_{\pi p} N_{p}+\sigma_{\pi n} N_{n}\right) N_{\pi} 
& =16 \sigma_{p p} N_{p} \varphi_{0} e^{-2 \sigma_{p p} N_{p} x}-\left(\frac{\lambda}{\gamma_{\pi} \beta_{\pi} c}+2 \sigma_{\pi p} N_{p}\right) N_{\pi} 
& =8 B \phi_{0} e^{-B x}-B^{\prime} N_{\pi}
\end{aligned}
$$
where $B=2 \sigma_{p p} N_{p}, B^{\prime}=2 \sigma_{\pi p} N_{p}+\lambda^{\prime}, \lambda^{\prime}=\frac{\lambda}{\gamma_{\pi} \beta_{\pi c}}$. The solution of the differential equation is
$$
N_{\pi}=\frac{8 B}{B^{\prime}-B}\left(e^{-B x}-e^{-B^{\prime} x}\right) \phi_{0} .
$$

Hence the number of charged pions which decay in the target per unit interval of $x$ is
$$
\frac{d N_{\pi}(\lambda)}{d x}=\frac{\lambda}{\gamma_{\pi} \beta_{\pi} c} N_{\pi}(\lambda)=\frac{8 B \lambda^{\prime}}{B^{\prime}-B}\left(e^{-B x}-e^{-B^{\prime} x}\right) \phi_{0} .
$$

Integration from $x=0$ to $x=\infty$ gives
$$
N_{\pi}(\lambda)=\frac{8 B \lambda^{\prime}}{B^{\prime}-B}\left(\frac{1}{B}-\frac{1}{B^{\prime}}\right) \phi_{0}=\frac{8 \lambda^{\prime} \phi_{0}}{B^{\prime}} .
$$

The branching ratio for $\pi \rightarrow \mu 
u \approx 100 \%$, so that $N_{\mu \pi} \approx N_{\pi}(\lambda)$. This means that the energy spectrum of muons is also flat (though in actual fact high-energy muons are more likely than low-energy ones), making the comparison with $N_{\mu c}$ much simpler.

Take for example $E_{\mu} \sim 100 \mathrm{GeV}$. Then $E_{\pi} \gtrsim 100 \mathrm{GeV}, \beta_{\pi} \approx 1, \gamma_{\pi} \gtrsim$ 714 , and so
$$
\lambda^{\prime}=\frac{\lambda}{\gamma_{\pi} \beta_{\pi} c}=\frac{1}{2.6 \times 10^{-8} \times 714 \times 3 \times 10^{10}}=1.8 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}
$$
As
$$
\begin{aligned}
\sigma_{\pi p} N_{p} & =20 \times 10^{-27} \times 2.34 \times 10^{24}=4.7 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1} \gg \lambda^{\prime} 
N_{\pi}(\lambda) & \simeq \frac{8 \lambda^{\prime} \phi_{0}}{2 \sigma_{\pi p} N_{p}}=\frac{8 \times 1.8 \times 10^{-6} \phi_{0}}{2 \times 4.7 \times 10^{-2}}=1.5 \times 10^{-4} \phi_{0}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence
$$
\frac{N_{\mu c}}{N_{\mu \pi}}=\frac{5.95 \times 10^{-7}}{1.5 \times 10^{-4}}=4 \times 10^{-3}
$$

raw_question_text: A small fish, four feet below the surface of Lake Mendota is viewed through a simple thin converging lens with focal length 30 feet. If the lens is 2 feet above the water surface (Fig. 1.8), where is the image of the fish seen by the observer? Assume the fish lies on the optical axis of the lens and that $n_{\text {air }}=1.0, n_{\text {water }}=1.33$.
normalized_question_text: A small fish, four feet below the surface of Lake Mendota is viewed through a simple thin converging lens with focal length 30 feet. If the lens is 2 feet above the water surface, where is the image of the fish seen by the observer? Assume the fish lies on the optical axis of the lens and that $n_{\text{air}}=1.0, n_{\text{water}}=1.33$.
reasoning_chain: An object at $P$ in water appears to be at $P^{\text{prime}}$ as seen by an observer in air, as Fig. 1.9 shows. The paraxial light emitted by $P$ is refracted at the water surface, for which 1.33 $\sin i_{1}=\sin i_{2}.$ As $i_{1}, i_{2}$ are very small, we have the approximation $1.33 i_{1}=i_{2}$. Also,$i_{2}=\alpha \approx \frac{\overline{\mathrm{OQ}}}{\overline{\mathrm{OP}}}, \quad i_{1}=\beta \approx \frac{\overline{\mathrm{OQ}}}{\overline{\mathrm{QP}}}.$ Hence, we have $\overline{\mathrm{OP}^{\prime}}=\frac{1}{1.33} \cdot \overline{\mathrm{OP}}=3 \mathrm{ft}$ Let the distance between the apparent location of the fish and the center of the lens be $u$, then $\nu=2+\overline{\mathrm{OP}^{\prime}}=5 \mathrm{ft}$ From $\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{u}+\frac{1}{v}$, we have $v=-6 \mathrm{ft}$ Therefore, the image of the fish is still where the fish is, four feet below the surface of Lake Mendota.

raw_question_text: As shown in Fig. 1.29, the image which would be cast by the converging lens alone has a distance of 0.5 cm between top and bottom. Calculate the position and size of the final image. Draw a ray diagram showing image formation for a point on the image not on the axis of the lenses. Using at least 2 rays. (The rays need not be the same for both lenses.) Explain how you arrive at the ray diagram.
normalized_question_text: As shown in Fig. 1.29, the image which would be cast by the converging lens alone has a distance of 0.5 cm between top and bottom. Calculate the position and size of the final image. Draw a ray diagram showing image formation for a point on the image not on the axis of the lenses, using at least 2 rays. (The rays need not be the same for both lenses.) Explain how you arrive at the ray diagram.
reasoning_chain:
An object at infinite distance away finds its image on the back focal plane of the converging lens, i.e., at $20 - 15 = 5\,\text{cm}$ behind the diverging lens. The Gaussian lens formula
$$
\frac{1}{u} + \frac{1}{v} = \frac{1}{f}
$$
applied to the diverging lens then gives for $u = -5\,\text{cm}$ and $f = -10\,\text{cm}$, $v = 10\,\text{cm}$. The lateral magnification is
$$
m = \frac{h}{h'} = \frac{10}{5} = 2.
$$

Hence the size of the final image is
$$
0.5 \times 2 = 1\,\text{cm}.
$$

normalized_question_text: A 35 mm camera lens of focal length 50 mm is made into a telephoto lens by placing a negative lens between it and the film, as shown (Fig. 1.43). $\mathrm{L}_{1}=$ camera lens, $f_{1}=50 \mathrm{~mm} ; \mathrm{L}_{2}=$ negative lens, $f_{2}=-100 \mathrm{~mm}$. What is the magnification produced by the lens combination?
reasoning_chain: The magnification of the lens combination is the product of that of each lens: $$\begin{aligned} M & =\left|\frac{v_{1}}{u_{1}}\right| \times\left|\frac{v_{2}}{u_{2}}\right|=\frac{\left[\frac{(500 \times 1600)}{(9 \times 29)}\right]}{\left[\frac{(500 \times 320)}{9}\right]} \\ & =\frac{5}{29}=0.17 \end{aligned}$$

raw_question_text:
A point source $S$ located at the origin of a coordinate system emits a spherical sinusoidal wave in which the electric field $E_{1}$ is given by $E_{1}=$ $A\left( rac{D}{r}ight) \cos \left(\omega t- rac{2 \pi r}{\lambda}ight)$, where $r$ is the distance from $S$. In addition, there is a plane wave propagating along the $x$-axis. This wave is given by
\[
E_{2}=A \cos \left(\omega t- rac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}ight) .
\]
(Note that we treat both $E_{1}$ and $E_{2}$ as scalar waves in this problem.) Both waves are incident on a flat screen perpendicular to the $x$-axis and at a distance $D$ from the origin, as shown in the figure (Fig. 2.1). Compute the resultant intensity $I$ at the screen as a function of the distance $y$ from the $x$-axis for values of $y$ small compared with $D$. Express $I$ in terms of $y, D, \lambda$ and the intensity $I_{0}$ at $y=0$.
normalized_question_text:
A point source $S$ located at the origin of a coordinate system emits a spherical sinusoidal wave in which the electric field $E_{1}$ is given by $E_{1}=$ $A\left( rac{D}{r}ight) \cos \left(\omega t- rac{2 \pi r}{\lambda}ight)$, where $r$ is the distance from $S$. In addition, there is a plane wave propagating along the $x$-axis. This wave is given by
\[
E_{2}=A \cos \left(\omega t- rac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}ight) .
\]
(Note that we treat both $E_{1}$ and $E_{2}$ as scalar waves in this problem.) Both waves are incident on a flat screen perpendicular to the $x$-axis and at a distance $D$ from the origin, as shown in the figure (Fig. 2.1). Compute the resultant intensity $I$ at the screen as a function of the distance $y$ from the $x$-axis for values of $y$ small compared with $D$. Express $I$ in terms of $y, D, \lambda$ and the intensity $I_{0}$ at $y=0$.
reasoning_chain:
The electric field $E$ at a point, distance $r$ from the origin, on the screen is given by
$$
\begin{aligned}
E &= E_{1} + E_{2} = A\left[\frac{D}{r} \cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi r}{\lambda}\right) + \cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi x}{\lambda}\right)\right] \\
&\approx A\left[\cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi r}{\lambda}\right) + \cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi x}{\lambda}\right)\right]
\end{aligned}
$$
where we have used the approximation $r \approx D$.

For $y \ll D$, we have
$$
r = \left(D^{2} + y^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} \approx D\left(1 + \frac{y^{2}}{2D^{2}}\right)
$$
and thus
$$
\begin{aligned}
E &= A\left[\cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi D\left(1 + \frac{y^{2}}{2D^{2}}\right)}{\lambda}\right) + \cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi D}{\lambda}\right)\right] \\
&= 2A \cos\left(\frac{\pi y^{2}}{D\lambda}\right) \cdot \cos\left(\omega t - \frac{2\pi D\left(1 + \frac{y^{2}}{4D^{2}}\right)}{\lambda}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence
$$
I \propto E^{2} \propto \cos^{2}\left(\frac{\pi y^{2}}{D\lambda}\right)
$$

Let $I = I_{0}$ at $y = 0$; we then have
$$
I = I_{0} \cos^{2}\left(\frac{\pi y^{2}}{D\lambda}\right)
$$

As the distance from the centre increases, the fringes become closer. This is similar to Fresnel's zone plate.

raw_question_text: A two-slit diffraction pattern is produced by the arrangement shown in Fig. 2.9. A discharge tube produces light of wavelength $\lambda$ which passes through a small slit $S$ immediately in front of the tube. The centers of the two slits of width $w$ are at distance $D$ apart. Find the condition that a maximum intensity be observed at the screen a distance $l$ from the central plane.
normalized_question_text: A two-slit diffraction pattern is produced by the arrangement shown in Fig. 2.9. A discharge tube produces light of wavelength $\lambda$ which passes through a small slit $S$ immediately in front of the tube. The centers of the two slits of width $w$ are at distance $D$ apart. Find the condition that a maximum intensity be observed at the screen a distance $l$ from the central plane.
reasoning_chain:
The intensity distribution on the screen produced by the elemental slit of width $dy$ of the slit sources is given by
$$
dI = 2I_{0}\left(\frac{\sin \beta}{\beta}\right)^{2}(1 + \cos \delta) dy
$$
where $\beta = \frac{\pi w l}{\lambda L} = \frac{\pi w \sin \theta}{\lambda}$ with $\sin \theta = \frac{l}{L}$, $\delta = \delta_{1} + \delta_{2}$ with
$$
\delta_{1} = \frac{2\pi D \sin \theta}{\lambda}, \quad \delta_{2} = \frac{2\pi D y}{\lambda x}
$$
and $I_{0}$ is a constant. Integration yields the intensity distribution produced by the slit source:
$$
\begin{aligned}
I &= 2I_{0}\left(\frac{\sin \beta}{\beta}\right)^{2} \int_{-\frac{s}{2}}^{\frac{s}{2}}(1 + \cos \delta) dy \\
&= 2I_{0} S\left(\frac{\sin \beta}{\beta}\right)^{2}\left\{1 + \frac{\sin\left(\frac{\pi D S}{\lambda x}\right)}{\left(\frac{\pi D S}{\lambda x}\right)} \cos\left(\frac{2\pi D}{\lambda} \sin \theta\right)\right\}
\end{aligned}
$$

If $\frac{2\pi D \sin \theta}{\lambda} = \frac{2\pi D l}{\lambda L} = 2n\pi$, i.e., $l = \frac{n\lambda L}{D}$, where $n$ is an integer, maximum intensity is observed on the screen at distance $l$ from the central plane.
clean_decision_rationale: The sample is understandable and otherwise well-aligned, but the provided formula uses ambiguous symbols (`I`/`L` vs the question's `l`), which creates a small answer-normalization risk for downstream annotation.

raw_question_text: A two-slit diffraction pattern is produced by the arrangement shown in Fig. 2.9. A discharge tube produces light of wavelength $\lambda$ which passes through a small slit $S$ immediately in front of the tube. The centers of the two slits of width $w$ are at distance $D$ apart. How large may $w$ be before the interference pattern is washed out?
normalized_question_text: A two-slit diffraction pattern is produced by the arrangement shown in Fig. 2.9. A discharge tube produces light of wavelength $\lambda$ which passes through a small slit $S$ immediately in front of the tube. The centers of the two slits of width $w$ are at distance $D$ apart. How large may $w$ be before the interference pattern is washed out?
reasoning_chain: The interference pattern is washed out for the first time if $\sin \beta=0$, or $\beta=\pi w l /(\lambda L)=\pi$, i.e., $w=\frac{\lambda L}{l}=D$. In general, $w=\frac{D}{n}$, or $n=\frac{D}{w}$.

raw_question_text: Light of wavelength $\lambda$ and intensity $I_{0}$ is incident perpendicularly onto an opaque circular disk of radius $R$. In each case, find the intensity of the light at a distance $L$ behind the obstacle, on the path passing through the center of the circle. Take $L \gg R$.
normalized_question_text: Light of wavelength $\lambda$ and intensity $I_{0}$ is incident perpendicularly onto an opaque circular disk of radius $R$. In each case, find the intensity of the light at a distance $L$ behind the obstacle, on the path passing through the center of the circle. Take $L \gg R$.
reasoning_chain:
For diffraction by the opaque circular disk, the field $A^{\prime}$ at $P$ is given by Babinet's principle
$$
A + A^{\prime} = A_{\infty} = A_{0} e^{i k L}
$$
where $A$ is the field at $P$ due to diffraction by the circular hole above, $A_{\infty}$ is the field due to diffraction by a hole of infinite size. Thus
$$
A^{\prime} = A_{0} e^{i k L} - A = A_{0} \exp\left[i k\left(L + \frac{R^{2}}{2 L}\right)\right]
$$
giving
$$
I^{\prime} \sim \left|A^{\prime}\right|^{2} = I_{0}
$$
i.e., the intensity is nearly the same as that without any diffracting screen provided the opaque obstacle is small.

raw_question_text:
A plane wave with wavenumber $k$ is incident on a slit of width $a$. The slit is covered by a transparent wedge whose thickness is proportional to the distance from the top of the slit $(t = \gamma x)$, see Fig.\ 2.36. The index of refraction of the transparent wedge is $n$. The intensity of light at an angle $\theta$ is given by
$$
I \propto \frac{\sin^2(\beta a)}{(\beta a)^2}
$$

Derive an expression for $\beta$ in terms of $k$, $n$, $\gamma$ and $\theta$.
normalized_question_text:
A plane wave with wavenumber $k$ is incident on a slit of width $a$. The slit is covered by a transparent wedge whose thickness is proportional to the distance from the top of the slit $(t = \gamma x)$, see Fig.\ 2.36. The index of refraction of the transparent wedge is $n$. The intensity of light at an angle $\theta$ is given by
$$
I \propto \frac{\sin^2(\beta a)}{(\beta a)^2}
$$

Derive an expression for $\beta$ in terms of $k$, $n$, $\gamma$ and $\theta$.
reasoning_chain:
For a wedge with thickness $t = \gamma x$, the apex angle (in radians) is $\gamma$ to first order approximation. The optical path difference introduced by the wedge causes an angular deviation of transmitted light given by:
$$
\delta = (n - 1)\gamma
$$

The single-slit diffraction intensity distribution follows the standard Fraunhofer pattern:
$$
I(\theta) = I_0 \left(\frac{\sin \beta}{\beta}\right)^2 
$$
where the diffraction parameter is:
$$
\beta \equiv \frac{\pi a}{\lambda} \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} a k \sin\theta
$$
with $k = 2\pi/\lambda$ being the wavenumber and $\theta$ the observation angle relative to the optical axis.

When the wedge prism covers the slit, the angular deviation modifies the diffraction pattern through coordinate transformation $\theta \to \theta - \delta$:
$$
I(\theta) = I_0 \left(
\frac{\sin\left[\frac{\pi a}{\lambda}\sin(\theta - \delta)\right]}
{\frac{\pi a}{\lambda}\sin(\theta - \delta)}
\right)^2
= I_0 \left(
\frac{\sin\left\{\frac{1}{2} a k \sin[\theta - (n-1)\gamma]\right\}}
{\frac{1}{2} a k \sin[\theta - (n-1)\gamma]}
\right)^2
$$

Key observations:
\begin{itemize}
\item The characteristic $\sinc^2$ intensity profile is preserved
\item The entire diffraction pattern shifts by $\delta = (n-1)\gamma$
\item The angular shift is wavelength-independent for small $\gamma$
\item Central maximum occurs when $\theta = (n-1)\gamma$
\end{itemize}

This results in the modified diffraction parameter:
$$
\beta = \frac{\pi a}{\lambda} \sin[\theta - (n-1)\gamma] = \frac{1}{2} a k \sin[\theta - (n-1)\gamma]
$$

raw_question_text: A pinhole camera consists of a box in which an image is formed on the film plane which is a distance $P$ from a pinhole of diameter $d$. The object is at a distance $L$ from the pinhole, and light of wavelength $\nabla$ is used (Fig. 2.66). normalized_question_text: A pinhole camera consists of a box in which an image is formed on the film plane which is a distance $P$ from a pinhole of diameter $d$. reasoning_chain:
For the images of two close points of separation $D$ on the object to be resolved, the distance between the two images should not be smaller than $\Delta_2$. Geometric optics gives
$$
\frac{D}{L} = \frac{\Delta_2}{P}.
$$
Hence 
$$
D = \sqrt{\frac{\Delta_2 L(L+P)}{P}}.
$$

raw_question_text: A beam of light, with $\lambda=5000 \AA$, traveling in the $z$-direction is polarised at $45^{\circ}$ to the $x$-direction. It passes through a Kerr cell, i.e., a substance such that $n_{x}-n_{y}=K E^{2}$, where $n_{x}$ and $n_{y}$ are the refractive indices for light polarized in the $x$ and $y$ directions. $E$ is the strength of an externally applied electric field in the $x$-direction. The cell has length 1 cm and $K=2.5 \times 10^{-6}$ (meter) $^{2} /(\text { volts })^{2}$. If the light, after passing through the Kerr cell, passes through a polarization analyzer whose plane of polarisation is perpendicular to that of the original beam, calculate the smallest value of $E$ which gives maximum transmission. Assume the effect of the electric field on reflection at the Kerr cell is negligible.
normalized_question_text: A beam of light, with $\lambda=5000 \AA$, traveling in the $z$-direction is polarised at $45^{\circ}$ to the $x$-direction. It passes through a Kerr cell, i.e., a substance such that $n_{x}-n_{y}=K E^{2}$, where $n_{x}$ and $n_{y}$ are the refractive indices for light polarized in the $x$ and $y$ directions. $E$ is the strength of an externally applied electric field in the $x$-direction. The cell has length 1 cm and $K=2.5 \times 10^{-6}$ (meter) $^{2} /(\text { volts })^{2}$. If the light, after passing through the Kerr cell, passes through a polarization analyzer whose plane of polarisation is perpendicular to that of the original beam, calculate the smallest value of $E$ which gives maximum transmission. Assume the effect of the electric field on reflection at the Kerr cell is negligible.
reasoning_chain:
The Kerr cell induces birefringence. As the direction of the electric field is $45^\circ$ to the direction of polarization of the light, the amplitudes of the components polarized parallel to the $x$ and $y$ directions are the same. When the phase difference $\delta$ between the components is
$$
\delta = \frac{2\pi l(n_{x}-n_{y})}{\lambda} = \frac{2\pi l K E^{2}}{\lambda} = (2j+1)\pi, \quad j=0 \text{ or integer},
$$
the Kerr cell acts as a half-wave plate and rotates the plane of polarization by $90^\circ$ so that it coincides with the transmission axis of the analyzer and gives maximum transmission. Putting $j=0$ yields the smallest $E$, $E_{\min}$, which is given by
$$
E_{\min}^{2} = \frac{\lambda}{2 K l} = 10~\mathrm{V}^{2}/\mathrm{m}^{2} \text{ for } l=1~\mathrm{cm}.
$$

reasoning_chain:
When Bragg's law
$$
2d \sin\theta = m\lambda \quad (m=1,2,3,\ldots)
$$
is satisfied, we get intense spots on the film. The wavelength $\lambda$ is given by
$$
\lambda = \sqrt{150/V}~\text{Å}.
$$
The smallest $\theta$ for film recording is given by $m=1$, for which
$$
\theta_{\min} = \arcsin\left(\frac{\lambda}{2d}\right) = \arcsin\left(\frac{1}{2d}\sqrt{\frac{150}{V}}\right).
$$

raw_question_text: A ruby laser emits light with a wavelength of $6943 \AA$, which to a very good approximation is a plane wave. What are the amplitudes of the electric and magnetic field vectors of this plane wave propagating through water, if the time-averaged power of the beam in the water is 100 milliwatts $/ \mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ?
normalized_question_text: A ruby laser emits light with a wavelength of $6943 \AA$, which to a very good approximation is a plane wave. What are the amplitudes of the electric and magnetic field vectors of this plane wave propagating through water, if the time-averaged power of the beam in the water is 100 milliwatts $/ \mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ?
reasoning_chain: For a plane electromagnetic wave, $\sqrt{\epsilon} E=\sqrt{\mu} H$. The time-averaged power of the beam per unit cross-sectional area is its intensity $$I=\langle E H\rangle=\sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}}\left\langle E^{2}\right\rangle=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{\epsilon}{\mu}} E_{0}^{2}=\frac{n}{2 c \mu} E_{0}^{2},$$ where $E_{0}$ is the amplitude of the electric field, $c$ the velocity of light in free space and $n$ the refractive index of water. The permeability of water is $$\mu \approx \mu_{0}=4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{H} / \mathrm{m} .$$ Hence $$\begin{array}{l} E_{0}=\sqrt{\frac{2 I c \mu}{n}}=7.5 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m} \\ B_{0}=\frac{E_{0}}{v}=\frac{E_{0} n}{c}=3.3 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~T}, \end{array}$$ $B_{0}$ being the amplitude of the magnetic induction.
clean_decision_rationale: The sample is fully understandable and complete as a text-based physics problem, with a complete answer, usable open rewrite, and strong alignment and solvability evidence. The lack of visual necessity is not a blocker for semantic usability.

raw_question_text: An undulator consists of a linear periodic array of magnets of alternating polarity. Each repeat unit in the array of magnets has length $d$ and there are $N$ such units. An electron of speed $v(v \text{sim} c)$, passing through this undulator, travels a path with only small deflections, as shown in Fig. 3.5. The motion of the electron along this path produces radiation which is most intense at a certain fundamental frequency corresponding to a wavelength $\text{lambda}$. Find the wavelength $\text{lambda}$ using the condition that this forward radiation from successive sections must interfere constructively.
normalized_question_text: An undulator consists of a linear periodic array of magnets of alternating polarity. Each repeat unit in the array of magnets has length $d$ and there are $N$ such units. An electron of speed $v(v \text{sim} c)$, passing through this undulator, travels a path with only small deflections, as shown in Fig. 3.5. The motion of the electron along this path produces radiation which is most intense at a certain fundamental frequency corresponding to a wavelength $\lambda$. Find the wavelength $\lambda$ using the condition that this forward radiation from successive sections must interfere constructively.
reasoning_chain:
The condition required is
$nk r-\omega t=k(r+d)-\omega\left(t+\frac{d}{v}\right)+2 \pi$
i.e.,
$\frac{c}{\lambda} \frac{d}{v}=\frac{d}{\lambda}+1$

Putting $\frac{v}{c}=\beta$, we have
$\lambda=d\left(\frac{1}{\beta}-1\right)$.

raw_question_text: Solar energy at the rate $800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ strikes a flat solar panel for water heating. If the panel has absorptance $=0.96$ for all wavelengths and the sides are perfect insulators, calculate the maximum temperature of the water.

\] 
where $\sigma=5.67 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \, \mathrm{K}^{4}$ is Stefan's constant, $\phi$ is the solar energy flux density, and $\alpha$ is the absorptance of the panel. With 
\[ 
\begin{aligned} 
\sigma & =5.67 \times 10^{-8} \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \, \mathrm{K}^{4}, \\ 
\alpha & =0.96, \\ 
\phi & =800 \, \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}, 
\end{aligned} 
\] 
we obtain $T=341 \, \mathrm{K}$ for the maximum temperature of the water.

reasoning_chain:

$$ 
T \propto \alpha^{1/4} 
$$ 
when $\alpha^{\prime} = \alpha/2$, we have 
$$ 
T^{\prime} = (1/2)^{1/4}T = 286\,\mathrm{K} 
$$ 
Thus if the absorptance dropped by $1/2$, the maximum temperature would be 286 K.

raw_question_text: An insulating spherical shell of radius $R$ carries surface charge density $\sigma(\theta)=\sigma_{o} \cos \theta$, where $\sigma_{o}$ is a constant. The shell is sent spinning around the $x$-axis $(\vec{\omega}=\omega \hat{x})$. Calculate the power radiated.
normalized_question_text: An insulating spherical shell of radius $R$ carries surface charge density $\sigma(\theta)=\sigma_{o} \cos \theta$, where $\sigma_{o}$ is a constant. The shell is sent spinning around the $x$-axis $(\vec{\omega}=\omega \hat{x})$. Calculate the power radiated.
reasoning_chain:
Solution 5.2. To find the power radiated, we can think of this as a dipole which is given by
\[
\vec{p}=p_{o}[\cos (\omega t) \hat{z}-\sin (\omega t) \hat{y}]
\]
and the power radiated is given by
\[
P=\frac{\mu_{o} \ddot{o}^{2}}{6 \pi c}
\]

Noting
\[
\ddot{\vec{p}}=-\omega^{2} \vec{p}
\]
it follows that $\ddot{p}^{2}=\omega^{4} p_{o}^{2}$. We must now find $p_{o}$ from the charge distribution using
\[
\vec{p}_{o}=\int \vec{r}^{\prime} \rho\left(\vec{r}^{\prime}\right) \mathrm{d} \tau^{\prime}=\int \vec{r}^{\prime} \sigma_{o} \cos \theta \mathrm{~d} a^{\prime}
\]

Since we are in spherical coordinates
\[
\vec{r}=R[\sin \theta(\cos \phi \hat{x}+\sin \phi \hat{y})+\cos \theta \hat{z}]
\]
and
\[
\mathrm{d} a=R^{2} \sin \theta \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta
\]

So
\[
\begin{aligned}
\vec{p}_{o}= & \sigma_{o} \int_{0}^{\pi} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} R^{3} \sin \theta \cos \theta[\sin \theta(\cos \phi \hat{x}+\sin \phi \hat{y})+\cos \theta \hat{z}] \mathrm{d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta \\
= & \sigma_{o} R^{3}\left[\hat{x} \int_{0}^{\pi} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} \sin ^{2} \theta \cos \theta \cos \phi \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta+\hat{y} \int_{0}^{\pi} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} \sin ^{2} \theta \cos \theta \sin \phi \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta\right. \\
& \left.+\hat{z} \int_{0}^{\pi} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} \sin \theta \cos ^{2} \theta \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta\right]
\end{aligned}
\]
The $\hat{x}$ and $\hat{y}$ integrals are both zero and
\[
\hat{z} \int_{0}^{\pi} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} \sin \theta \cos ^{2} \theta \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} \theta=\frac{4 \pi}{3} \hat{z}
\]

Therefore,
\[
\vec{p}_{o}=\sigma_{o} \frac{4 \pi}{3} R^{3} \hat{z}
\]
and
\[
\ddot{p}^{2}=\omega^{4} p_{o}^{2}=\omega^{4} \sigma_{o}^{2} \frac{16 \pi^{2}}{9} R^{6}
\]

The power radiated is then given by
\[
P=\frac{\mu_{o} \ddot{p}^{2}}{6 \pi c}=\frac{\mu_{o}}{6 \pi c} \omega^{4} \sigma_{o}^{2} \frac{16 \pi^{2}}{9} R^{6}=\frac{8 \pi \mu_{o} \sigma_{o}^{2} \omega^{4} R^{6}}{27 c}
\]

raw_question_text: 系统如图示，小滑轮固定不动，轻绳与滑轮间无摩擦，小物块 $A, B$ 质量相同，$B$ 与水平桌面间无摩擦。开始时 $A, B$ 静止，右侧绳段与水平桌面间的夹角为 $30^{\circ}$ 。今将系统自由释放，通过计算，判断释放后一瞬间物块 $B$ 是否会向上离开水平桌面？
normalized_question_text: 系统如图示,小滑轮固定不动,轻绳与滑轮间无摩擦,小物块 $A, B$ 质量相同,$B$ 与水平桌面间无摩擦。开始时 $A, B$ 静止,右侧绳段与水平桌面间的夹角为 $30^{\circ}$ 。今将系统自由释放,通过计算,判断释放后一瞬间物块 $B$ 是否会向上离开水平桌面?
reasoning_chain:
$A$ 下行加速度记为 $a_{A}, B$ 左行加速度记为 $a_{B}$ ，将绳中张力记为 $T$ ，则有 $m g-T=m a_{A}, T \cos \theta_{0}=m a_{B} . \quad m: A, B$ 各自质量因 $\theta_{0}=30^{\circ}$ 时，$v_{B}=0$ ，故有运动关联式
$$
a_{B} \cos \theta_{0}=a_{A} .
$$

解得
$$
T=m g /\left(1+\cos ^{2} \theta_{0}\right), T \sin \theta_{0}=\left.\frac{\sin \theta_{0}}{1+\cos ^{2} \theta_{0}} m g\right|_{\theta_{0}=30^{\circ}}=\frac{2}{7} m g<m g,
$$

故 $B$ 不会向上离开桌面。
clean_decision_rationale: The question and answer are complete, the diagram-text combination is clear enough to identify the pulley setup and 30-degree condition, and the rewritten variant is usable as-is. Solvability and multimodal support are strong, with no serious failure indicating the sample is unusable for annotation.

raw_question_text: Consider an infinite sheet in the $x y$-plane carrying surface current $\\vec{K}(t)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & t \leqslant 0 \\ K \\hat{x}, & t>0 \end{array}\right.$. Find the generated magnetic field.
normalized_question_text: Consider an infinite sheet in the $xy$-plane carrying surface current $\vec{K}(t)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}0,& t\leqslant 0\\K\hat{x},& t>0\end{array}\right.$. Find the generated magnetic field.
reasoning_chain:
Solution 4.7. Since $\rho=0$, we have $V=0$. At a point P in the figure below, we have vector potential
\[
\vec{A}=\frac{\mu_{o}}{4 \pi} \int \frac{K \hat{x}}{r} \mathrm{~d} a=\frac{\mu_{o} K \hat{x}}{4 \pi} \int \frac{1}{r} \mathrm{~d} a
\]

Note that for a time $t<\frac{z}{c}$, the news that the current has been turned on has not arrived yet. So, for $t \geqslant \frac{z}{c}$, the area contained in
\[
r^{2} \leqslant(c t)^{2}-z^{2} \Rightarrow r \leqslant \sqrt{(c t)^{2}-z^{2}}
\]
is the only part of the sheet that contributes to the fields. We can rewrite our potential as
\[
\vec{A}=\frac{\mu_{o} K \hat{x}}{4 \pi} \int_{0}^{\sqrt{(c t)^{2}-z^{2}}} \int_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{r}{r} \mathrm{~d} \phi \mathrm{~d} r
\]
where $r=\sqrt{z^{2}+r^{2}}$. Therefore,
\[
\vec{A}=\frac{\mu_{o} K \hat{x}}{4 \pi} 2 \pi \int_{0}^{\sqrt{(c t)^{2}-z^{2}}} \frac{r}{\sqrt{z^{2}+r^{2}}} \mathrm{~d} r=\frac{\mu_{o} K \hat{x}}{2}(c t-z)
\]

Now our electric field is given by
\[
\vec{E}=-\frac{\partial \vec{A}}{\partial t}=-\frac{\mu_{o} K \hat{x}}{2} \frac{\partial}{\partial t}(c t-z)=-\frac{\mu_{o} c K \hat{x}}{2}
\]
and our magnetic field is given by
\[
\begin{aligned}
\vec{B} & =\nabla \times \vec{A}=\frac{\mu_{o} K}{2}[\nabla \times[\hat{x}(c t-z)]] \\
& =\frac{\mu_{o} K}{2} \frac{\partial}{\partial z}(c t-z) \hat{y}=-\frac{\mu_{o} K}{2} \hat{y}
\end{aligned}
\]
which is what we would expect in the static case.
clean_decision_rationale: The sample is understandable, solvable, and the rewrite is usable, so it should not be rejected just because the image is unnecessary. However, the target answer is risky because the question asks for the magnetic field of an infinite current sheet without clearly specifying region/sign convention or whether a fuller time-dependent field is expected, while the provided answer gives only a single vector expression.

possible_answer_ambiguity_about_field_on_each_side_of_sheet

raw_question_text: 如力图 7.24.1 所示，质量为 $M$ 的小球两侧分别用劲度系数均为 $k$ 的弹簧与两个质量均为 $m$ 的小球连成一条直线，水平放置，忽略一切阻力和摩擦力。试求特征频率和振动模式。
normalized_question_text: 如力图 7.24.1 所示,质量为 $M$ 的小球两侧分别用劲度系数均为 $k$ 的弹簧与两个质量均为 $m$ 的小球连成一条直线,水平放置,忽略一切阻力和摩擦力。试求特征频率和振动模式。
reasoning_chain:
【解】如力图7.24.1所示，取三球连线为$x$轴，设某时刻$t$三球偏离平衡位置的位移为$x_1,x_2,x_3$，运动方程为
$$
\begin{cases} 
m\ddot{x}_1 = k(x_2 - x_1) \\
M\ddot{x}_2 = k(x_1 - 2x_2 + x_3) \\
m\ddot{x}_3 = -k(x_3 - x_2)
\end{cases}
$$

设简谐振动解：
$$
x_i = A_i\cos\omega t \quad (i=1,2,3)
$$
代入得振幅方程：
$$
\begin{cases}
(k-m\omega^2)A_1 - kA_2 = 0 \\
-kA_1 + (2k-M\omega^2)A_2 - kA_3 = 0 \\
-kA_2 + (k-m\omega^2)A_3 = 0
\end{cases}
$$

特征频率满足：
$$
\begin{vmatrix}
k-m\omega^2 & -k & 0 \\
-k & 2k-M\omega^2 & -k \\ 
0 & -k & k-m\omega^2
\end{vmatrix} = 0
$$

解得特征频率：
$$
\omega = 0,\ \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}},\ \sqrt{\frac{M+2m}{Mm}k}
$$
其中$\omega=0$为平移解，舍去。

振动模式分析：

1. 当$\omega_1=\sqrt{\dfrac{k}{m}}$时：
$$
A_2 = 0,\quad A_1 = -A_3
$$
（$M$球静止，两侧$m$球反相振动）

2. 当$\omega_2=\sqrt{\dfrac{M+2m}{Mm}k}$时：
$$
A_1 = A_3,\quad A_2 = -\dfrac{2m}{M}A_3
$$
（两侧$m$球同相振动，$M$球反相振动）

raw_question_text: 图示的电路中，$t<0$ 时，电容器充电过程已完成。$t=0$ 时，接通电键 K 。将接通前瞬间时刻记为 $t=0^{-}$，接通后瞬间时刻记为 $t=0^{+} . t \geqslant 0^{+}$时，按图中虚线所示方向设定各路电流 $I_{1}, I_{2}, I_{C}$ 和 $I_{3}$ 的流向。导出电容器上方极板电量 $Q(0^{+})$；
normalized_question_text: 图示的电路中,$t<0$ 时,电容器充电过程已完成。$t=0$ 时,接通电键 K 。将接通前瞬间时刻记为 $t=0^{-}$,接通后瞬间时刻记为 $t=0^{+} . t \geqslant 0^{+}$时,按图中虚线所示方向设定各路电流 $I_{1}, I_{2}, I_{C}$ 和 $I_{3}$ 的流向。导出电容器上方极板电量 $Q(0^{+})$;
reasoning_chain:
在 $t=0^{-}$到 $t=0^{+}$的无穷短的 $\mathrm{d} t$ 时间内，电容器上方极板电量 $Q$ 可能会变化，此种变化必定通过有限电流 $I_{C}$ 实现，增量必为无穷小量。无穷小增量 $\mathrm{d} Q$ ，使
$$
U_{A B}(0^{+})=U_{A B}(0^{-})=\frac{R_{2}}{R_{1}+R_{2}} \varepsilon
$$

成立，便得
$$
\begin{array}{c}
I_{1}(0^{+})=\left[\varepsilon-U_{A B}(0^{+})\right] / R_{1}=\frac{\varepsilon}{R_{1}+R_{2}}=I_{1}(0^{-}), 
I_{2}(0^{+})=U_{A B}(0^{+}) / R_{2}=\frac{\varepsilon}{R_{1}+R_{2}}=I_{2}(0^{-}), 
I_{3}(0^{+})=U_{A B}(0^{+}) / R_{3}=\frac{R_{2}}{R_{3}\left(R_{1}+R_{2}\right)^{\varepsilon},} 
I_{C}(0^{+})=I_{1}(0^{+})-I_{2}(0^{+})-I_{3}(0^{+})=-I_{3}(0^{+})=-\frac{R_{2}}{R_{3}\left(R_{1}+R_{2}\right)^{\varepsilon} .}
\end{array}
$$

因
$$
\mathrm{d} Q=I_{C}(0^{+}) \mathrm{d} t=-\frac{R_{2}}{R_{3}\left(R_{1}+R_{2}\right)} \varepsilon \mathrm{d} t<0, \quad \text { 且为无穷小量, }
$$

故
$$
Q(0^{+})=Q(0^{-})+\mathrm{d} Q=Q(0^{-})=\frac{R_{2}}{R_{1}+R_{2}} C \varepsilon .
$$

可见，在 $t=0^{-}$到 $t=0^{+}$的无穷短时间内，$I_{C}(0^{+})$与图示方向相反，成为新增的 $I_{3}(0^{+})$电流。

raw_question_text: 一长 $l=0.40~\mathrm{m}$ 的均匀木棒，质量 $m'=1.00~\mathrm{kg}$，可绕水平轴 $O$ 在坚直平面内转动，开始时棒自然坚直地悬垂。现有质量 $m=8~\mathrm{g}$ 的子弹以 $v=200~\mathrm{m/s}$ 的速率从 $A$ 点射入棒中，假定 $A$ 点与 $O$ 点的距离为 $\frac{3l}{4}$。求棒开始运动时的角速度。

reasoning_chain: 取子弹和棒为系统。碰撞前系统对轴的角动量就是作匀速直线运动的子弹对轴的角动量，为 $L_{0}=\frac{3}{4}l\,m\,v$. 碰撞后系统对轴的角动量为 $L=J\omega=\left[m\left(\frac{3}{4}l\right)^2+\frac{1}{3}m'l^2\right]\omega$. $\omega$ 为子弹射入棒后二者开始共同运动的角速度。因角动量守恒，有 $L=L_{0}$. 得 $\omega=\frac{3mvl}{4J}=8.89~\mathrm{rad/s}$.

raw_question_text: 如图所示的打桩装置，半径为 $R$ 的带齿轮转盘绕中心轴的转动惯量为 $J$ ，转动角速度为 $\omega_{0}$ ，夯锤的质量为 $m$ ，开始处于静止状态，当转盘与夯锤碰撞后，问夯锤的速度能有多大？
normalized_question_text: 如图所示的打桩装置，半径为 $R$ 的带齿轮转盘绕中心轴的转动惯量为 $J$，转动角速度为 $\omega_{0}$，夯锤的质量为 $m$，开始处于静止状态。当转盘与夯锤碰撞后，问夯锤的速度能有多大？
reasoning_chain: 设碰撞后夯锤与转盘有相同的角速度 $\omega$ ，将夯锤视作质点。由系统的角动量守恒可知得 $J \omega_{0}=(m R^{2}+J) \omega$ ，解得 $\omega=\frac{J \omega_{0}}{m R^{2}+J}$ 。则夯锤的速度为 $v=R \omega=\frac{J R \omega_{0}}{m R^{2}+J}$

raw_question_text: 1 mol氦气（理想气体）经历如热图2.19.1所示的循环过程，热图2.19.1中$AB$,$BC$,$CA$均为直线，有关参数已经标明，试求循环效率$\eta$。
normalized_question_text: 1 mol氦气(理想气体)经历如热图2.19.1所示的循环过程,热图2.19.1中$AB$,$BC$,$CA$均为直线,有关参数已经标明,试求循环效率$\eta$。
reasoning_chain:
【解】由热力学第一定律，对$CA$中任一元过程，因氦气为1 mol，有
$$
\mathrm{d}Q = \mathrm{d}U + p \mathrm{d}V = C_V \mathrm{d}T + p \mathrm{d}V = \frac{3}{2} R \mathrm{d}T + p \mathrm{d}V
$$

状态方程为
$$
pV = RT
$$

$CA$过程方程为
$$
p = \alpha - \beta V
$$

因$p_C = 2p_1$, $V_C = V_1$, $p_A = p_1$, $V_A = 2V_1$，得$\alpha = 3p_1$, $\beta = \frac{p_1}{V_1}$，故过程方程为
$$
p = 3p_1 - \frac{p_1}{V_1} V
$$

式(1)~式(3)联立，消去$p$和$T$，得
$$
\mathrm{d}Q = \frac{p_1}{2V_1} (15V_1 - 8V) \mathrm{d}V
$$

在转折点（$D$点）
$$
\mathrm{d}Q = 0
$$

故$D$点的体积为
$$
V_D = \frac{15}{8} V_1
$$

在$CD$过程中，$V < V_D$, $\mathrm{d}Q > 0$，吸热；在$DA$过程中，$V > V_D$, $\mathrm{d}Q < 0$，放热。把式(4)代入式(3)和式(2)，得
$$
p_D = \frac{9}{8} p_1, \quad RT_D = \frac{9}{8} p_1 \cdot \frac{15}{8} V_1 = \frac{135}{64} p_1 V_1
$$

循环过程$ABCA$的效率为
$$
\eta = \frac{W}{Q_1}
$$

式中：$W$是循环过程对外所做总功，即为$\triangle ABC$的面积，有
$$
W = \frac{1}{2} p_1 V_1
$$

式中：$Q_1$是循环过程中总的净吸热，为
$$
Q_1 = Q_{BC} + Q_{CD}
$$

其中
$$
\begin{aligned}
Q_{BC} &= C_V (T_C - T_B) = \frac{3}{2} (RT_C - RT_B) \\
&= \frac{3}{2} (2p_1 V_1 - p_1 V_1) = \frac{3}{2} p_1 V_1 \\
Q_{CD} &= \Delta U_{CD} + W_{CD} = C_V (T_D - T_C) + \text{梯形面积} \\
&= \frac{3}{2} (RT_D - RT_C) + \frac{1}{2} \left(2p_1 + \frac{9}{8} p_1\right) \left(\frac{7}{8} V_1\right) \\
&= \frac{3}{2} \left(\frac{9}{8} p_1 \cdot \frac{15}{8} V_1 - 2p_1 V_1\right) + \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{25}{8} p_1 \cdot \frac{7}{8} V_1 \\
&= \frac{21}{128} p_1 V_1 + \frac{175}{128} p_1 V_1 = \frac{49}{32} p_1 V_1
\end{aligned}
$$

于是
$$
\begin{aligned}
Q_1 &= Q_{BC} + Q_{CD} = \left(\frac{3}{2} + \frac{49}{32}\right) p_1 V_1 = \frac{97}{32} p_1 V_1 \\
\eta &= \frac{W}{Q_1} = \frac{\frac{1}{2} p_1 V_1}{\frac{97}{32} p_1 V_1} = \frac{16}{97} \approx 0.16
\end{aligned}
$$

证明在理想气体的任何准静态过程中，吸热与放热的转折点是$p-V$图上绝热曲线与该过程曲线的切点。

任意元过程可看作多方过程。热力学第一定律为
$$
\mathrm{d}Q = \mathrm{d}U + \mathrm{d}W
$$

其中
$$
\mathrm{d}U = \frac{M}{\mu} C_V \mathrm{d}T, \quad \mathrm{d}W = p \mathrm{d}V = \frac{M}{\mu} \frac{RT}{V} \mathrm{d}V
$$

利用多方过程方程
$$
T V^{n-1} = \text{常量}, \quad \text{得} \quad \mathrm{d}W = -\frac{M}{\mu} \frac{R}{n-1} \mathrm{d}T
$$

于是元过程中吸热的公式为
$$
\mathrm{d}Q = \frac{M}{\mu} \left(C_V - \frac{R}{n-1}\right) \mathrm{d}T = \frac{M}{\mu} C_V \frac{n - \gamma}{n - 1} \mathrm{d}T
$$

式中：$n$是多方指数。式(5)表明$\mathrm{d}Q$的正、负取决于$\mathrm{d}T$及$\frac{n - \gamma}{n - 1}$两个因子。

如热图2.19.3所示，经$A$点作等体$(n = \infty)$、绝热$(n = \gamma)$、等温$(n = 1)$、等压$(n = 0)$曲线，它们把$p-V$平面分成八个区域，从$A$点出发的任意元过程必在八区之中或恰在边界上，

从$A \rightarrow$(1), $\gamma < n < \infty$, $\mathrm{d}T > 0$，故$\mathrm{d}Q > 0$，吸热。
从$A \rightarrow$(2), $n < 0$, $\mathrm{d}T > 0$，故$\mathrm{d}Q > 0$，吸热。
从$A \rightarrow$(3), $0 < n < 1$, $\mathrm{d}T > 0$，故$\mathrm{d}Q > 0$，吸热。
从$A \rightarrow$(4), $1 < n < \gamma$, $\mathrm{d}T < 0$，故$\mathrm{d}Q > 0$，吸热。
从$A \rightarrow$(5),(6),(7),(8)类似，只是$\mathrm{d}T$反号，故$\mathrm{d}Q < 0$，放热。
若过程反向，则结论相反，例如从(1)$\rightarrow A$，放热。余类似。

总之，如热图2.19.3所示，在$p-V$图上，以经过$A$点的绝热曲线为界，从$A$点向绝热曲线右方的任意元过程均吸热，从$A$点向绝热曲线左方的任意元过程均放热。换言之，对于任何过程，其吸热与放热的转折点是该过程曲线与绝热曲线的切点。因为如果该过程曲线在绝热曲线左侧，则从上方经该过程到达切点应吸热，从切点经该过程往下应放热，所以切点是转折点；如果该过程在绝热曲线右侧或过程反向，可作类似讨论，切点仍是转折点。

normalized_question_text: 一矩形线圈长 $l=20\,\mathrm{cm}$,宽 $b=10\,\mathrm{cm}$,由 100 匝表面绝缘的导线绕成,放置在一根长直导线的旁边,并和直导线在同一平面内,该长直导线是一个闭合回路的一部分,其余部分离线圈很远,其影响可略去不计。求习题 9-24 图(a)情况下,线圈与长直导线间的互感。
reasoning_chain: 解：（a）取坐标轴 $Ox$ 垂直于长直电流向右为正，坐标原点位于长直电流处。设长直电流为 $I$，在 $x$ 处的磁感应强度为 $B=\frac{\mu_{0} I}{2\pi x}$. 通过矩形线圈的磁通链为 $\Psi_{a}=N\int_{S}\boldsymbol{B}\cdot\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{S}=\int_{b}^{2b}N\frac{\mu_{0} I}{2\pi x}l\,\mathrm{d}x=N\frac{\mu_{0} I l}{2\pi}\ln2$. 互感系数为 $M_{a}=\frac{\Psi_{a}}{I}=N\frac{\mu_{0} l}{2\pi}\ln2=2.8\times10^{-6}\,\mathrm{H}$.

During the motion, the velocity $v$ of the ball remains perpendicular to the rod and can be orthogonally decomposed into a horizontal component $v_{/\text{}}$ and a remaining component $v_\text{}$ within the plane perpendicular to the rod. During the motion, when the angle between the rod and the $z$-axis is $\theta$ ($\theta > 0$), determine the values of $v_{/\text{}}$ and $v_\text{}$ as shown in Figure 2, and provide the values of $v_{/\text{}}$ and $v_{\perp}$ when $\theta = \frac{\pi}{2}$;

The $Oxy$ coordinate plane represents a horizontal geometric plane (not a physical table or ground but a plane through which the rod can pass). During the motion, the velocity $v$ of the ball remains perpendicular to the rod and can be orthogonally decomposed into a horizontal component $v_{/\text{}}$ and a remaining component $v_\text{}$ within the plane perpendicular to the rod. During the motion, when the angle between the rod and the $z$-axis is $\theta$ ($\theta > 0$), determine the values of $v_{/\text{}}$ and $v_\text{}$ as shown in Figure 2, and provide the values of $v_{/\text{}}$ and $v_{\perp}$ when $\theta = \frac{\pi}{2}$;

When $\theta=\frac{\pi}{2}$, we have $$v_{\top}=v_{0} \sin \theta_{0}, \quad v_{\perp}=\sqrt{\left(v_{0}^{2} \cos \theta_{0}+2 g l\right) \cos \theta_{0}}.$$

raw_question_text: 如图所示，$N \geqslant 2$ 块相同的导体平板相互间隔地平行放置，各自带电量分别为 $Q_{1}, Q_{2}, \cdots Q_{N}$ 。静电平衡后，试求：第 1 块平板左侧面电荷 $Q_{18}$ 和第 $N$ 块平板右侧面电荷 $Q_{\text {N右；}}$
normalized_question_text: 如图所示，$N \geqslant 2$ 块相同的导体平板相互间隔地平行放置，各自带电量分别为 $Q_{1}, Q_{2}, \cdots Q_{N}$。静电平衡后，试求：第 1 块平板左侧面电荷 $Q_{18}$ 和第 $N$ 块平板右侧面电荷 $Q_{\text{N右；}}$。
reasoning_chain:
解 (1) 静电平衡后，各板内部场强均为零。取题解图中用虚线所示的高斯面，可证得（过程略）第 1 块板的右侧面电量
$$
Q_{1 \text{右}} + Q_{2 \text{左}} = 0.
$$

同理可得
$$
Q_{2 \text{右}} + Q_{3 \text{左}} = 0,\ \cdots,\ Q_{N-1 \text{右}} + Q_{N \text{左}} = 0.
$$

又因
$$
\begin{aligned}
& Q_{1 \text{左}} + \left[\left(Q_{1 \text{右}} + Q_{2 \text{左}}\right) + \left(Q_{2 \text{右}} + Q_{3 \text{左}}\right) + \cdots + \left(Q_{N-1 \text{右}} + Q_{N \text{左}}\right)\right] + Q_{N \text{右}} 
= & Q_{1} + Q_{2} + \cdots + Q_{N},
\end{aligned}
$$

即得
$$
Q_{1 \text{左}} + Q_{N \text{右}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} Q_{i}.
$$

考虑到 $(Q_{1 \text{右}}, Q_{2 \text{左}})$，$(Q_{2 \text{右}}, Q_{3 \text{左}})$，$\cdots$，$(Q_{N-1 \text{右}}, Q_{N \text{左}})$ 中每一组面电荷给各块平板内部场强贡献均为零，便要求面电荷组 $(Q_{1 \text{左}}, Q_{N \text{右}})$ 给各块平板内部场强贡献也为零，即要求
$$
Q_{1 \text{左}} = Q_{N \text{右}},
$$

即得
$$
Q_{1 \text{左}} = Q_{N \text{右}} = \frac{1}{2} \sum_{i=1}^{N} Q_{i}.
$$

(2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
Q_{1 \text{左}} &= \frac{1}{2} \sum_{i=1}^{N} Q_{i}, 
Q_{1 \text{右}} &= Q_{1} - Q_{1 \text{左}} = \frac{1}{2}\left(Q_{1} - \sum_{i=1}^{N} Q_{i}\right), 
Q_{2 \text{左}} &= -Q_{1 \text{右}} = \frac{1}{2}\left(-Q_{1} + \sum_{i=2}^{N} Q_{i}\right), 
Q_{2 \text{右}} &= Q_{2} - Q_{2 \text{左}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[(Q_{1} + Q_{2}) - \sum_{i=3}^{N} Q_{i}\right], 
&\cdots 
Q_{N-1 \text{左}} &= -Q_{N-2 \text{右}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[-\left(Q_{1} + Q_{2} + \cdots + Q_{N-2}\right) + \left(Q_{N-1} + Q_{N}\right)\right], 
Q_{N-1 \text{右}} &= Q_{N-1} - Q_{N-1 \text{左}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[\left(Q_{1} + Q_{2} + \cdots + Q_{N-1}\right) - Q_{N}\right], 
Q_{N \text{左}} &= -Q_{N-1 \text{右}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[-\left(Q_{1} + Q_{2} + \cdots + Q_{N-1}\right) + Q_{N}\right], 
Q_{N \text{右}} &= Q_{N} - Q_{N \text{左}} = \frac{1}{2} \sum_{i=1}^{N} Q_{i}.
\end{aligned}
$$

raw_question_text: Find the self-inductance $L$ per unit length for an infinitely long coaxial cable of radii $a, b$ with $a<b$, carrying a current $I$, as in the figure. Use energy calculations.
normalized_question_text: Find the self-inductance $L$ per unit length for an infinitely long coaxial cable of radii $a, b$ with $a<b$, carrying a current $I$, as in the figure. Use energy calculations.
reasoning_chain:
The self-inductance is related to the energy as
\[
W=\frac{L I^{2}}{2}
\]

Let us calculate the energy of the configuration
\[
W=\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}} \int_{V} B^{2} \mathrm{~d} \tau
\]

Next, we need to find the magnetic field. From Ampère's law, the only significant (non-zero magnetic field region) is for radius $s$ between $a$ and $b$.
\[
\oint \vec{B} \cdot \mathrm{~d} \vec{\ell}=\mu_{o} I_{\mathrm{enc}}
\]
where $I_{\text {enc }}=I$ is the enclosed current. The left-hand side is given by
\[
\oint \vec{B} \cdot \mathrm{~d} \vec{\ell}=B 2 \pi s
\]

From here,
\[
\vec{B}=\frac{\mu_{o} I}{2 \pi s} \hat{\phi}
\]

The energy of a cylindrical shell of length $\ell$, radius $s$ and thickness $\mathrm{d} s$ is:
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{d} W & =\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}} B^{2} 2 \pi \ell s \mathrm{~d} s \\
\mathrm{~d} W & =\frac{1}{2 \mu_{o}}\left(\frac{\mu_{o} I}{2 \pi s}\right)^{2} 2 \pi \ell s \mathrm{~d} s \\
\mathrm{~d} W & =\frac{\mu_{o} I^{2} \ell}{4 \pi} \frac{\mathrm{~d} s}{s}
\end{aligned}
\]

We obtain the total energy by integrating over $s$ from $a$ to $b$
\[
W=\int_{a}^{b} \frac{\mu_{o} I^{2} \ell}{4 \pi} \frac{\mathrm{~d} s}{s}=\left.\frac{\mu_{o} I^{2} \ell}{4 \pi} \ln s\right|_{a} ^{b}=\frac{\mu_{o} I^{2} \ell}{4 \pi} \ln \frac{b}{a}
\]

Back to impedance per unit length:
\[
W=\frac{L I^{2}}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{L}{\ell}=\frac{2 W}{I^{2} \ell}=\frac{\mu_{o}}{2 \pi} \ln \frac{b}{a}
\]

raw_question_text: 1 mol 单原子分子理想气体所经准静态循环过程是如热图 2.21.1 所示的圆。有参量已在热图 2.21.1 中标明。 试求一次循环对外所做的功 $W$ 。
normalized_question_text: 1 mol 单原子分子理想气体所经准静态循环过程是如热图 2.21.1 所示的圆。有参量已在热图 2.21.1 中标明。 试求一次循环对外所做的功 $W$ 。
reasoning_chain:
【解】循环过程为$p-V$图上的圆，过程方程为
$$
\left(\frac{p}{p_0}-2\right)^2 + \left(\frac{V}{V_0}-2\right)^2 = 1
$$

式中：$p_0=10^5\,\text{N/m}^2$, $V_0=10^{-3}\,\text{m}^3$。若改变$p,V$轴的标度，循环过程为椭圆，其过程方程为
$$
\frac{(p-2p_0)^2}{p_0^2} + \frac{(V-2V_0)^2}{V_0^2} = 1
$$

就代数方程而言，式(1)和式(2)是等价的。一次循环$ABCDA$对外做功$W$为圆或椭圆的面积，为
$$
W = \pi p_0 V_0 = 314\,\text{J}
$$

raw_question_text: 如力图 4.3.1 所示，设质量为 $M$ 的恒星固定不动。质量为 $m$ 的行星在其引力作用下运动，已知距恒星 $\boldsymbol{r}_{0}$ 时的速度为 $v_{0}$ ，且 $\boldsymbol{v}_{0}$ 的方向与径矢垂直。试讨论：行星轨迹分别为抛物线，椭圆，圆和双曲线时，$v_{0}$ 应取何值？
normalized_question_text: 如力图 4.3.1 所示,设质量为 $M$ 的恒星固定不动。质量为 $m$ 的行星在其引力作用下运动,已知距恒星 $\boldsymbol{r}_{0}$ 时的速度为 $v_{0}$ ,且 $\boldsymbol{v}_{0}$ 的方向与径矢垂直。试讨论:行星轨迹分别为抛物线,椭圆,圆和双曲线时,$v_{0}$ 应取何值?
reasoning_chain:
【解】如力图4.3.1所示，行星绕固定恒星运行的总机械能为
$$
E = \frac{1}{2} m v_{0}^{2} - \frac{G m M}{r_{0}}
$$
式中：$v_{0}$ 的取值不同，$E$ 也不同，行星轨迹的类别也各异。

1. $E = 0$ 时为抛物线。此时行星到达无穷远时速度变为零，即刚好能逃逸恒星的束缚，相应的 $v_{0}$ 值称为逃逸速度，用 $v_{\mathrm{e}}$ 表示。由式（1），$v_{\mathrm{e}}$ 满足
$$
\frac{1}{2} m v_{\mathrm{e}}^{2} - \frac{G m M}{r_{0}} = 0, \quad \text{即} \quad v_{\mathrm{e}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 G M}{r_{0}}}
$$

2. $E < 0$ 时一般为椭圆轨道。由式（1），$v_{0}$ 应满足
$$
\frac{1}{2} m v_{0}^{2} - \frac{G m M}{r_{0}} < 0, \quad \text{即} \quad v_{0} < \sqrt{\frac{2 G M}{r_{0}}} = v_{\mathrm{e}}
$$

圆轨道是椭圆轨道的特殊情形。在椭圆轨道的情形下，本章题1中的方程（10）有两个实根，即 $\rho_{\max}$ 和 $\rho_{\min}$ ，它们对应两个取极值的半径 $r_{\min}$ 和 $r_{\max}$（因 $\rho = 1 / r$）。由本章题1中的式（11），有
$$
\frac{1}{r_{\min}} + \frac{1}{r_{\max}} = \frac{2 G m^{2} M}{L^{2}}, \quad \text{即} \quad r_{\max} + r_{\min} = r_{\max} r_{\min} \frac{2 G m^{2} M}{L^{2}}
$$

注意到，如力图4.3.2所示，有
$$
r_{\max} + r_{\min} = 2 a
$$
式中：$a$ 是椭圆的半长轴，故有
$$
2 a = r_{\max} r_{\min} \frac{2 G m^{2} M}{L^{2}}
$$

由本章题1中的式（12），即
$$
\frac{1}{r_{\max} r_{\min}} = -\frac{2 m E}{L^{2}}
$$
故有
$$
2 a = -\frac{G m M}{E}
$$

式（2）表明，椭圆轨道的半长轴完全由总能量 $E$ 决定。将式（1）代入式（2），得
$$
\frac{1}{2 a} = \frac{1}{r_{0}} - \frac{v_{0}^{2}}{2 G M}
$$

当椭圆退化成圆时，有
$$
r_{0} = a
$$
将行星取圆轨道时的 $v_{0}$ 表为 $v_{\mathrm{c}}$ ，则 $v_{\mathrm{c}}$ 应满足
$$
\frac{1}{r_{0}} - \frac{1}{2 r_{0}} = \frac{v_{\mathrm{c}}^{2}}{2 G M}
$$
即
$$
v_{\mathrm{c}}^{2} = \frac{G M}{r_{0}}, \quad v_{\mathrm{c}} = \sqrt{\frac{G M}{r_{0}}} = \frac{v_{\mathrm{e}}}{\sqrt{2}}
$$

3. $E > 0$ 时为双曲线。行星具有正能量，意即逃逸到无穷远时动能不为零。由式（1），$v_{0}$ 满足
$$
\frac{1}{2} m v_{0}^{2} - \frac{G m M}{r_{0}} > 0, \quad \text{即} \quad v_{0} > \sqrt{\frac{2 G M}{r_{0}}} = v_{\mathrm{e}}
$$

总之，当 $v_{0} > v_{\mathrm{e}}$ 时轨道为双曲线；$v_{0} = v_{\mathrm{e}}$ 时为抛物线；$v_{\mathrm{c}} < v_{0} < v_{\mathrm{e}}$ 时为（在圆轨道外面的）椭圆；$v_{0} = v_{\mathrm{c}}$ 时为圆；$v_{0} < v_{\mathrm{c}}$ 时为（在圆轨道里面的）椭圆。以上各种轨道一并在力图4.3.3中画出，以便比较。

raw_question_text: 如图所示，置于地面和塔顶的两架弹射器，以相同的速率 $v_{0}$ 沿它们的连线同时弹射出两球。如果塔顶高 $h=10\,\mathrm{m}$ ，两弹射器相隔的水平距离 $s=20\,\mathrm{m}$ ，这两个球会不会在空中相碰？
normalized_question_text: 如图所示,置于地面和塔顶的两架弹射器,以相同的速率 $v_{0}$ 沿它们的连线同时弹射出两球。如果塔顶高 $h=10\,\mathrm{m}$ ,两弹射器相隔的水平距离 $s=20\,\mathrm{m}$ ,这两个球会不会在空中相碰?
reasoning_chain:
取坐标系 $xOy$ ，如解图 1－16 所示。设两球在被弹射后的 $t_{C}$ 时刻相碰点的坐标为 $(x_{C}, y_{C})$。在水平方向有
$$x_{C}= rac{s}{2}=(v_{0}\cos	heta)t_{C}$$
得
$$t_{C}= rac{s}{2v_{0}\cos	heta}$$
$t$ 时刻两球在竖直方向的坐标分别为
$$y_{1}=(v_{0}\sin	heta)t- rac{1}{2}gt^{2}$$
$$y_{2}=h-(v_{0}\sin	heta)t- rac{1}{2}gt^{2}$$
将上述式子分别代入，并由 $s	an	heta=h$ ，可以得到
$$y_{1}=y_{2}=y_{C}$$
$t_{C}$ 时刻两球的垂直坐标和水平坐标相同，表明两球在空中相碰是可能的

reasoning_chain: 在这个平面内，对 e 光运用折射定律，有 $\sin i=n_{e} \sin r_{e}$ 式中 $i$ 为空气中的人射角，$r_{e}$ 为 e 光在晶体内的折射角，据题图可知 $r_{e}=30^{\circ}$ 。可得 $i=\arcsin \left(n_{e} \sin r_{e}\right)=\arcsin \left(1.49 \times \sin 30^{\circ}\right)=0.84 \mathrm{rad}=48^{\circ} 10^{\prime}$

reasoning_chain: 取坐标系 $xOy$, 原点 $O$ 位于球的发射处，如解图 1－15 所示。乒乓球（质点）的轨迹方程为 $y=x\tan\theta_{0}-\frac{g x^{2}}{2v_{0}^{2}\cos^{2}\theta_{0}}$. 代入桌边点的坐标：$y=0, x=a$, 得 $v_{0}^{2}\sin2\theta_{0}=ga$. 代入落地点的坐标：$y=-h, x=a+b$, 得 $2h\cos^{2}\theta_{0}+(a+b)v_{0}^{2}\sin2\theta_{0}=(a+b)^{2}g$. 解得 $\tan\theta_{0}=\frac{ah}{(a+b)b}$ 代入数据可得 $\tan\theta_{0}=\frac{8}{5},\quad \theta_{0}=58^{\circ}$.

raw_question_text: 有一半径为 $R$ 的圆线圈，通有电流 $I_{1}$ ，另有一通有电流 $I_{2}$ 的无限长直导线，垂直于圆线圈平面放置，如图所示。设圆线圈可绕 $y$ 轴转动。试求圆线圈在图示的位置时所受到的磁力矩；

reasoning_chain: 如解图 8－48 所示，在圆线圈上 $P$ 点处取电流元 $I_{1} \mathrm{~d} l$ ，长直电流 $I_{2}$ 在 $P$ 点处的磁感应强度大小为 $B=\frac{\mu_{0} I_{2}}{2 \pi r}$。电流元 $I_{1} \mathrm{~d}\boldsymbol{l}$ 所受安培力 $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{F}$ 的方向垂直纸面向外，其大小为 $\mathrm{d} F=I_{1} B \mathrm{~d} l \sin \theta=\frac{\mu_{0} I_{1} I_{2}}{2 \pi r} \sin \theta \mathrm{~d} l$。若在 $y$ 轴左侧的圆线圈上取一与 $P$ 点对称的电流元，其受力 $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{F}^{\prime}$ 与 $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{F}$ 的大小相等，方向相反，形成一对力偶，使圆线圈可绕 $y$ 轴转动。$\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{F}$ 对 $y$ 轴的力矩 $\mathrm{d}\boldsymbol{M}$ 向下，大小为 $\mathrm{d} M=x \mathrm{~d} F \sin \frac{\pi}{2}=\frac{\mu_{0} I_{1} I_{2}}{2 \pi} \sin ^{2} \theta \mathrm{~d} l$，式中 $x=r \sin \theta$。由图示几何关系可知，$\mathrm{d} l=R \mathrm{~d} \alpha, \alpha=2 \theta$，$\mathrm{d} M$ 可表示为 $\mathrm{d} M=\frac{\mu_{0} R I_{1} I_{2}}{4 \pi}(1-\cos \alpha) \mathrm{d} \alpha$。所以，圆线圈所受到的磁力矩为 $M=2 \int \mathrm{~d} M=\frac{\mu_{0} R I_{1} I_{2}}{2 \pi} \int_{0}^{\pi}(1-\cos \alpha) \mathrm{d} \alpha=\frac{1}{2}\mu_{0} R I_{1} I_{2}$。

$$
q \omega r_{0} B=m \omega^{2} r_{0},
$$

$$
\omega=q B / m .

raw_question_text: 如力图5.7.1 所示，$A B, B C, C D$ 和 $D E$ 为质量可略的等长细线，长度均为 5 m ，$A$ 端和 $E$ 端悬挂在水平天花板上，$A E=14 \text{ m} . B$ 和 $D$ 是质量均为 $m_{0}=7 \text{ kg}$ 的相同小球。质量为 $m$ 的重物挂于 $C$ 点，平衡时 $C$ 点离天花板的垂直距离为 7 m 。试求质量 $m$ 。
normalized_question_text: 如力图5.7.1 所示,$A B, B C, C D$ 和 $D E$ 为质量可略的等长细线,长度均为 5 m ,$A$ 端和 $E$ 端悬挂在水平天花板上,$A E=14 \text{ m} . B$ 和 $D$ 是质量均为 $m_{0}=7 \text{ kg}$ 的相同小球。质量为 $m$ 的重物挂于 $C$ 点,平衡时 $C$ 点离天花板的垂直距离为 7 m 。试求质量 $m$ 。
reasoning_chain:
【解】1.如力图 5.7.3 所示，线段 $C B$ 和 $C D$ 的拉力相同，设为 $T_{1}$，线段 $D E$ 的拉力设为 $T_{2}$。由 $C$ 点受力平衡，有
$$
2 T_{1} \cos \alpha=m g
$$

式中：$\cos \alpha=3 / 5$（见分析），故
$$
T_{1}=\frac{5}{6} m g
$$

由 $D$ 点受力平衡，有
$$
\begin{array}{l}
T_{1} \sin \alpha=T_{2} \cos \alpha \\
T_{2} \sin \alpha=T_{1} \cos \alpha+m_{0} g
\end{array}
$$

因
$$
\cos \alpha=\frac{3}{5}, \quad \sin \alpha=\frac{4}{5}
$$

由式（1）$\sim$ 式（4），得
$$
4 T_{1}=3 T_{2}, \quad \frac{4}{5} T_{2}=\frac{3}{5} T_{1}+m_{0} g
$$

解出 $T_{1}=15 m_{0} g / 7$，故
$$
m=\frac{18}{7} m_{0}=18 \text{ kg}
$$
clean_decision_rationale: 题干、答案和配图共同构成完整可解的物理题，重写结果可直接用于标注；虽然对齐记录有1处冲突，但整体一致性、可验证性和可解性都很高，不构成实质性风险。

\(\mathrm{d}  oldsymbol{E}_{P}= rac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}}  rac{\mathrm{d}q}{r_{p}^{\prime 2}} i= rac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}}  rac{\lambda \mathrm{d}x}{\left( rac{l}{2}+d-xight)^{2}}  oldsymbol{i}\)

\( oldsymbol{E}_{P}=\int \mathrm{d} oldsymbol{E}_{P}= rac{\lambda}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}}\int_{- rac{1}{2}}^{ rac{l}{2}}  rac{\mathrm{d}x}{\left( rac{l}{2}+d-xight)^{2}}  oldsymbol{i}= rac{\lambda l}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}(d+l)d} i\)

reasoning_chain: 细杆对 $O$ 轴的转动惯量为 $J_{1}=\int_{0}^{m} l^{2}\,\mathrm{d}m=\int_{0}^{6r}\lambda l^{2}\,\mathrm{d}l=72\lambda r^{3}=12mr^{2}$. 圆盘对质心（圆心）轴的转动惯量为 $J_{2C}=\frac{1}{2}(4m)r^{2}=2mr^{2}$. 由平行轴定理，圆盘对 $O$ 轴的转动惯量为 $J_{2}=J_{2C}+(4m)(7r)^{2}=198mr^{2}$. 钟摆对端点 $O$ 轴的转动惯量为 $J=J_{1}+J_{2}=210mr^{2}$.

raw_question_text: 直角三角板 $A B C$ 的边长 $B C=a, A C=b$ ，开始时 $A B$ 边靠在 $y$ 轴上，$B$ 与坐标原点 $O$重合。今使 $A$ 点单调地沿 $y$ 轴负方向朝 $O$ 点移动，$B$ 点单调地沿 $x$ 轴正方向移动，如图 1所示。最终 $A$ 点到达 $O$ 点，$A B$ 边倒在 $x$ 轴上，如图 2 所示。试求三角板从图 1 到图 2 的移动过程中，$C$ 点经过的路程 $s_{C}$ 。
normalized_question_text: 直角三角板 $ABC$ 的边长 $BC=a, AC=b$。开始时 $AB$ 边靠在 $y$ 轴上，$B$ 与坐标原点 $O$ 重合。今使 $A$ 点单调地沿 $y$ 轴负方向朝 $O$ 点移动，$B$ 点单调地沿 $x$ 轴正方向移动。最终 $A$ 点到达 $O$ 点，$AB$ 边倒在 $x$ 轴上。试求三角板从初始位置到最终位置的移动过程中，$C$ 点经过的路程 $s_C$。
reasoning_chain:
解 路程与运动轨道相关，运动轨道由过程态（即过程中的各个状态）确定。取过程态如题解图所示，可以看出 $O, A, B, C$ 四点共圆。图中标以 $\alpha$ 的两个角因对应同一圆弧而相等，$C O$ 与 $x$ 轴夹角 $\alpha$ 便是定值，过程中 $C$ 必沿此连线作直线运动。引入图示 $v_{A}$ 和 $v_{C}$ ，标量化为 $v_{A}$ 和 $v_{C}$ 。其中 $v_{A}$ 始终为正；$v_{C}$ 取正时，$v_{C}$ 指向 $O$ 点，$v_{C}$ 取负时，$v_{C}$ 背离 $O$ 点。参考同一圆弧对应的两个 $\beta$ 角，$v_{A}$ 和 $v_{C}$ 沿 $C A$ 边方向分量相等的条件可表述成
$$
v_{A} \cos [\pi-(\alpha+\beta)]=v_{C} \sin \beta,
$$

得
$$
v_{C}=-\frac{\cos (\alpha+\beta)}{\sin \beta} v_{v_{A}}\left\{\begin{array}{l}
<0, \text { 当 } \alpha<\alpha+\beta<\frac{\pi}{2}, 
=0, \text { 当 } \alpha+\beta=\frac{\pi}{2}, 
>0, \text { 当 } \frac{\pi}{2}<\alpha+\beta<\alpha+\frac{\pi}{2},
\end{array}\right.
$$

可见 $C$ 点开始时沿直线背离 $O$ 点运动，到达 $\alpha+\beta=\frac{\pi}{2}$ 位置时停下，而后沿直线指向 $O$ 点运动，一直图 2 所示位置为止。据此得
$$
s_{C}=2 \sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}-(a+b) .
$$

raw_question_text: A uniform thin rod of length $l$ and resistance $R$ has its end $A$ constrained to move along a vertical smooth rail, and its end $B$ constrained to move along a horizontal smooth rail. The resistance of the rails can be neglected. Assume there is a uniform magnetic field in the direction shown in the figure. Initially, the rod's orientation angle is $\phi=0$. When the rod is released from rest, it falls and reaches an orientation angle $\phi$, at which point the downward velocity of end $A$ is measured to be $v_{A}$. Calculate the angular acceleration $\beta$ of the rod when $\phi=45^{\degree}$.
normalized_question_text: A uniform thin rod of length $l$ and resistance $R$ has its end $A$ constrained to move along a vertical smooth rail, and its end $B$ constrained to move along a horizontal smooth rail. The resistance of the rails can be neglected. Assume there is a uniform magnetic field in the direction shown in the figure. Initially, the rod's orientation angle is $\phi=0$. When the rod is released from rest, it falls and reaches an orientation angle $\phi$, at which point the downward velocity of end $A$ is measured to be $v_A$. Calculate the angular acceleration $\beta$ of the rod when $\phi=45^{\circ}$.
reasoning_chain:
因导轨法向作用力（求知量）对质心力矩不为零，故不采用质心轴转动定理求解 $\beta$ ，改用能量方法导出 $\beta$ 满足的关系式。
$\phi$ 角位置时，细杆动能为
$$
E_{\mathrm{k}}=\frac{1}{2} I_{M} \omega^{2}=\frac{1}{6} m l^{2} \omega^{2}, \quad \text { (瞬时轴 } I_{M}=\frac{1}{3} m l^{2} \text { ) }
$$
$t \sim t+\mathrm{d} t$ 时间间隔对应 $\phi \sim \phi+\mathrm{d} \phi$ ，有
$$
\mathrm{d} E_{\mathrm{k}}=\frac{1}{3} m l^{2} \omega \frac{\mathrm{~d} \omega}{\mathrm{~d} t} \mathrm{~d} t=\frac{1}{3} m l^{2} \beta \mathrm{~d} \phi . \quad(\omega \mathrm{d} t=\mathrm{d} \phi)
$$

重力势能减少量 $-\mathrm{d} W_{g}=\mathrm{d}\left[m g \frac{l}{2}(1-\cos \phi)\right]=m g \frac{l}{2} \sin \phi \mathrm{~d} \phi$ 。
电阻上消耗能量 $\quad \mathrm{d} W_{I}=P_{I} \mathrm{~d} t=\frac{B^{2} l^{2}}{4 R} v_{A}^{2} \frac{\cos ^{2} 2 \phi}{\sin ^{2} \phi} \mathrm{~d} t \quad\left(v_{A}=\omega l \sin \phi\right)$
$$
\begin{array}{l}
=\frac{B^{2} l^{2}}{4 R} \omega^{2} l^{2} \cos ^{2} 2 \phi \cdot \mathrm{~d} t \quad(\omega \mathrm{~d} t=\mathrm{d} \phi) 
=\frac{B^{2} l^{4}}{4 R} \omega \cos ^{2} 2 \phi \cdot \mathrm{~d} \phi
\end{array}
$$
由功能关系
$$
\mathrm{d} E_{\mathrm{k}}=-\mathrm{d} W_{\mathrm{g}}-\mathrm{d} W_{1},
$$

得
$$
\frac{1}{3} m l^{2} \beta=m g \frac{l}{2} \sin \phi-\frac{B^{2} l^{4}}{4 R} \omega \cos ^{2} 2 \phi,
$$
$\phi=45^{\circ}$ 时， $\cos ^{2} 2 \phi=0$ ，即得
$$
\beta=\frac{3 g}{2 l} \sin \phi=\frac{3 \sqrt{2}}{4 l} g,
$$

raw_question_text: 如图所示，航天飞机 $P$ 开始时沿 $A = B$ 的椭圆轨道绕地球巡航。$P$ 在轨道任一位置均可通过短时间点火喷气来改变速度大小和方向，使其进入圆轨道航行。规定将速度增大简称为“加速”，将速度减小简称为“减速”。设航天飞机主体携带一个太空探测器在偏心率 $e=$ $\sqrt{3} / 2$（偏心率 $e=C / A, C$ 为椭圆半焦距，$A$ 为椭圆半长轴）的椭圆轨道上航行到图中 $D$ 位置时，因为朝后发射探测器而使航天飞机主体进人圆轨道航行，探测器相对地球恰好沿抛物线轨道远去，试求航天飞机主体质量 $m_{1}$ 与探测器质量 $m_{2}$ 的比值 $\gamma(\gamma=$ $\left.m_{1}: m_{2}\right)$.
normalized_question_text: 如图所示，航天飞机 $P$ 开始时沿 $A = B$ 的椭圆轨道绕地球巡航。$P$ 在轨道任一位置均可通过短时间点火喷气来改变速度大小和方向，使其进入圆轨道航行。规定将速度增大简称为“加速”，将速度减小简称为“减速”。设航天飞机主体携带一个太空探测器在偏心率 $e=\sqrt{3}/2$（偏心率 $e=C/A$，$C$ 为椭圆半焦距，$A$ 为椭圆半长轴）的椭圆轨道上航行到图中 $D$ 位置时，因为朝后发射探测器而使航天飞机主体进入圆轨道航行，探测器相对地球恰好沿抛物线轨道远去，试求航天飞机主体质量 $m_{1}$ 与探测器质量 $m_{2}$ 的比值 $\gamma$（$\gamma=m_{1}:m_{2}$）。
reasoning_chain:
在 $D$ 处航天飞机主体和探测器原速度大小同为
$$
v_{0}=\frac{A-C}{B} \sqrt{\frac{G M}{A}} .
$$

主体与探测器分离时各自的速度大小分别记为 $v_{1}$ 与 $v_{2}$ ，则应有
圆运动，$\Rightarrow v_{1}=\sqrt{G M /(A+C)}$ ，
抛物线轨道运动 $\frac{1}{2} m_{2} v_{2}^{2}-G \frac{M m_{2}}{A+C}=0, \quad \Rightarrow \quad v_{2}=\sqrt{2 G M /(A+C)}$ ．
由分离过程动量守恒方程
$$
m_{1} v_{1}-m_{2} v_{2}=\left(m_{1}+m_{2}\right) v_{0}, m_{1}=\gamma m_{2},
$$

得
$$
\gamma=\left(v_{2}+v_{0}\right) /\left(v_{1}-v_{0}\right),
$$

即
$$
\gamma=\frac{\sqrt{\frac{2}{A+C}}+\frac{A-C}{B \sqrt{A}}}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{A+C}}-\frac{A-C}{B \sqrt{A}}},
$$

由 $e=\sqrt{3} / 2$ ，得
$$
C=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} A, B=\frac{1}{2} A \text {; 或 } A=2 B, C=\sqrt{3} B \text {, }
$$

代人上式，得
$$
\gamma=\frac{2+\sqrt{2-\sqrt{3}}}{\sqrt{2}-\sqrt{2-\sqrt{3}}}=2.808
$$

reasoning_chain: 设轮齿的啮合时间为 $\Delta t$ ，相互作用力为 $F$ 。啮合后 A 轮的角速度为 $\omega_{1}$ ， B 轮的角速度为 $\omega_{2}$ 。根据角动量定理，有 $$ -r_{1} F \Delta t=J_{1}(\omega_{1}-\omega_{0}) $$ $$ r_{2} F \Delta t=J_{2} \omega_{2} $$ 两轮边缘的线速度相同 $$ r_{1} \omega_{1}=r_{2} \omega_{2} $$ 解以上各式，得 $$ \omega_{1}=\frac{J_{1} \omega_{0} r_{2}^{2}}{J_{1} r_{2}^{2}+J_{2} r_{1}^{2}}, \quad \omega_{2}=\frac{J_{1} \omega_{0} r_{1} r_{2}}{J_{1} r_{2}^{2}+J_{2} r_{1}^{2}} $$

raw_question_text: 一轻绳绕过一定滑轮，滑轮轴光滑，滑轮的质量为 $m/4$ ，均匀分布在其边缘上。绳子的一端有一质量为 $m$ 的人 A 抓住了绳端，而在绳的另一端系了一质量为 $m/2$ 的重物 B。设人从静止开始相对绳以匀速向上爬时，绳与滑轮间无相对滑动，求重物 B 上升的加速度？（已知滑轮对过滑轮中心且垂直于轮面的轴的转动惯量 $J=mR^{2}/4$。）
normalized_question_text: 一轻绳绕过一定滑轮,滑轮轴光滑,滑轮的质量为 $m/4$ ,均匀分布在其边缘上。绳子的一端有一质量为 $m$ 的人 A 抓住了绳端,而在绳的另一端系了一质量为 $m/2$ 的重物 B。设人从静止开始相对绳以匀速向上爬时,绳与滑轮间无相对滑动,求重物 B 上升的加速度?(已知滑轮对过滑轮中心且垂直于轮面的轴的转动惯量 $J=mR^{2}/4$。)
reasoning_chain:
解 1：人相对绳子往上运动时，其对地加速度可能是向下的。设人对地加速度的大小为 $a$ ，方向向下，重物对地的加速度 $a$ 向上。 设定滑轮的半径为 $R$ 作逆时针转动，角加速度为 $\alpha$ 。两边绳的拉力分别为 $F_{\mathrm{T}1}$ 和 $F_{\mathrm{T}2}$ 。人，重物和滑轮的受力及力矩分析如解图 3－19 所示，分别有
\[
 egin{array}{c}
m_{A}g-F_{\mathrm{T}1}=m_{A}a \
F_{\mathrm{T}2}-m_{B}g=m_{B}a \
RF_{\mathrm{T}1}-RF_{\mathrm{T}2}=J\alpha \
a=R\alpha
\end{array}
\]
式中 $J= rac{m}{4}R^{2},\; m=m_{A}=2m_{B}$ 。解上述方程，得
\[
a= rac{2}{7}g
\]
$ a>0$ 表明加速度的方向与假定方向一致，即重物的加速度是向上的。

解 2 ：取人，轻绳，重物和滑轮为系统。系统所受外力对滑轮轴的力矩为
\[
M=m_{A}gR-m_{B}gR= rac{1}{2}mgR
\]
设人相对绳的速度为 $u$ ，重物相对地的上升速度为 $v$ ，则人相对地的速度为 $(u-v)$ 。系统对滑轮轴的角动量为
\[
L=m_{B}vR+J\omega-m_{A}(u-v)R=mR\left( rac{7}{4}v-uight)
\]
由角动量定理 $M= rac{\mathrm{d}L}{\mathrm{d}t}$ ，有
\[
 rac{1}{2}mgR= rac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d}t}\left[mR\left( rac{7}{4}v-uight)ight]= rac{7}{4}mR rac{\mathrm{d}v}{\mathrm{d}t}
\]
所以，重物 B 上升的加速度为 $a= rac{\mathrm{d}v}{\mathrm{d}t}= rac{2}{7}g$ 
两种解法的结果一致。

raw_question_text: 一同轴电缆，由半径为 $a$ 的导体圆柱芯线及内，外半径分别为 $b$ 和 $c$ 的同轴导体圆筒组成，如图所示。筒与柱间有相对磁导率为 $\mu_{\mathrm{r}}$ 的磁介质，导体圆柱和圆筒的磁导率近似为 $\mu_{0}$ 。电缆工作时，电流由圆柱流入，沿圆筒济回，而且在导体横截面上电流是均匀分布的。试求一段长为 $l$ 的电缆所储存的磁场能量。
normalized_question_text: 一同轴电缆，由半径为 $a$ 的导体圆柱芯线及内、外半径分别为 $b$ 和 $c$ 的同轴导体圆筒组成，如图所示。筒与柱间有相对磁导率为 $\mu_{\mathrm{r}}$ 的磁介质，导体圆柱和圆筒的磁导率近似为 $\mu_{0}$。电缆工作时，电流由圆柱流入，沿圆筒济回，而且在导体横截面上电流是均匀分布的。试求一段长为 $l$ 的电缆所储存的磁场能量。
reasoning_chain: 设电缆工作时的电流为 $I$，磁感应强度的分布由安培环路定理得到：$B=\begin{cases}\frac{\mu_{0}Ir}{2\pi a^{2}},\quad r\leq a,\\ \frac{\mu_{0}\mu_{\mathrm{r}}I}{2\pi r},\quad a<r\leq b,\\ \frac{\mu_{0}I}{2\pi r}\left(1-\frac{r^{2}-b^{2}}{c^{2}-b^{2}}\right),\quad b<r\leq c,\\ 0,\quad r>c.\end{cases}$ 磁能密度为 $w_{m}=\frac{B^{2}}{2\mu}$，体积元为 $dV=2\pi rl\,dr$。在三个区域内积分得：$W_{m1}=\frac{\mu_{0}I^{2}l}{4\pi a^{4}}\int_{0}^{a}r^{3}dr=\frac{\mu_{0}I^{2}l}{16\pi}$，$W_{m2}=\frac{\mu_{0}\mu_{\mathrm{r}}I^{2}l}{4\pi}\int_{a}^{b}\frac{dr}{r}=\frac{\mu_{0}\mu_{\mathrm{r}}I^{2}l}{4\pi}\ln\frac{b}{a}$，$W_{m3}=\frac{\mu_{0}I^{2}l}{4\pi}\int_{b}^{c}\left(\frac{c^{2}-r^{2}}{c^{2}-b^{2}}\right)^{2}\frac{dr}{r}=\frac{\mu_{0}I^{2}l}{16\pi(c^{2}-b^{2})^{2}}\left(4c^{4}\ln\frac{c}{b}-3c^{4}+4b^{2}c^{2}-b^{4}\right)$. 综合得到 $W_{m}=W_{m1}+W_{m2}+W_{m3}=\frac{\mu_{0}I^{2}l}{16\pi}\left[1+4\mu_{\mathrm{r}}\ln\frac{b}{a}+\frac{1}{(c^{2}-b^{2})^{2}}\left(4c^{4}\ln\frac{c}{b}-3c^{4}+4b^{2}c^{2}-b^{4}\right)\right]$.

raw_question_text: 如力图 7.21 .1 所示，质量为 $M$ ，长为 $L$ 的均匀刚性细杆的一端悬挂，可在铅垂面内绕悬点无摩擦地摆动。质量为 $m=M / 3$ 的小虫相对杆以速度 $v$ 缓慢地沿杆向下爬行，开始时，杆静止并与竖直线夹角为 $\theta_{0}$（设 $\theta_{0}$ 很小）；小虫位于杆上端的悬点，放开后，杆开始摆动，小虫开始爬行，试求：小虫沿杆爬行 $l$ 距离时，杆摆动的圆频率；
normalized_question_text: 如力图7.21.1所示，质量为$M$、长为$L$的均匀刚性细杆的一端悬挂，可在铅垂面内绕悬点无摩擦地摆动。质量为$m=M/3$的小虫相对杆以速度$v$缓慢地沿杆向下爬行。开始时，杆静止并与竖直线夹角为$\theta_0$（设$\theta_0$很小）；小虫位于杆上端的悬点。放开后，杆开始摆动，小虫开始爬行。试求：小虫沿杆爬行$l$距离时，杆摆动的圆频率。
reasoning_chain:
【解】1．当小虫沿杆爬行到任意位置 $l$ 处时，杆和虫系统的转动惯量为
$$
I = \frac{1}{3} M L^{2} + m l^{2} = \frac{1}{3} M L^{2} + \frac{1}{3} M l^{2} = \frac{M}{3}(L^{2} + l^{2})
$$

当虫在 $l$ 处且杆的摆角为任意的 $\theta$ 时，杆和虫系统受到的重力矩 $M_{\text{重}}$ 为
$$
M_{\text{重}} = -M g \frac{L}{2} \sin \theta - m g l \sin \theta
$$

由角动量定理，得
$$
\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} t}(I \dot{\theta}) = -M g \frac{L}{2} \sin \theta - m g l \sin \theta
$$

把 $I$ 代入，得
$$
\frac{M}{3} \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} t}\left[(L^{2} + l^{2}) \dot{\theta}\right] = -M g \sin \theta\left(\frac{L}{2} + \frac{l}{3}\right)
$$

即
$$
\frac{M}{3}(L^{2} + l^{2}) \ddot{\theta} + \frac{2}{3} M l \dot{l} \dot{\theta} = -\frac{3L + 2l}{6} M g \sin \theta
$$

因小虫爬行缓慢，$v = \dot{l}$ 很小，式（1）左边第二项可略。因摆角 $\theta$ 很小，$\sin \theta \approx \theta$。得
$$
\ddot{\theta} + \frac{l + \frac{3}{2}L}{L^{2} + l^{2}} g \theta = 0
$$

这就是虫爬行到 $l$ 处时，杆和虫系统的运动方程，它表明杆，虫的摆动是简谐振动，其圆频率为
$$
\omega = \sqrt{\frac{l + \frac{3}{2}L}{L^{2} + l^{2}} g}
$$

raw_question_text: 介质块缓慢进人空气平行板电容器过程中外力和电源作功问题。图示电路中空气平行板电容器可处理为真空平行板电容器，电键 K 合上后，充电过程已完成。图中外力 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 以朝右为正方向画出， $\boldsymbol{F}$ 的真实方向也可能朝左。若将电键 K 断开，利用图示外力 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 让介质块缓慢地全部进人电容器两极板之间，试求全过程 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 作功量 $A_{1}$ ；
normalized_question_text: 介质块缓慢进人空气平行板电容器过程中外力和电源作功问题。图示电路中空气平行板电容器可处理为真空平行板电容器，电键 K 合上后，充电过程已完成。图中外力 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 以朝右为正方向画出，$\boldsymbol{F}$ 的真实方向也可能朝左。若将电键 K 断开，利用图示外力 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 让介质块缓慢地全部进人电容器两极板之间，试求全过程 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 作功量 $A_1$。
reasoning_chain:
对充电过程已完成，电键 $K$ 未断开的初态，引人下述物理量，其含义不言自明．
\[
C_{0}=\frac{\varepsilon_{0} S}{d}, Q_{0}=C_{0} U_{0}, W_{0}=\frac{1}{2} Q_{0} U_{0}=\frac{Q_{0}^{2}}{2 C_{0}}=\frac{1}{2} C_{0} U_{0}^{2}.
\]
介质块缓慢进入空气平行板电容器过程中外力和电源作功问题。图示电路中空气平行板电容器可处理为真空平行板电容器，电键 $K$ 合上后，充电过程已完成。图中外力 $\boldsymbol{F}$ 以朝右为正方向画出，$\boldsymbol{F}$ 的真实方向也可能朝左。$K$ 未断开，介质块缓慢进入过程中，极板电量缓慢变化（类似于热学中的准静态过程），电路中电流强度 $I$ 及电流密度 $j$ 均可略，电阻 $R$ 内的电场强度 $E=\rho j$（$\rho$：电阻率）也可略。无论从宏观上或微观上考察，都是电阻两端电势差为零，电阻不消耗能量。全过程中，电源电压 $U_{0}$ 全部加在电容上，电源通过流出电量作功输出的能量不会被电阻消耗一部分，而是全部输向电容。介质块全部进入后，相应地导出下述物理量：

\begin{align*}
C &= \varepsilon_{\text{r}} C_{0}, \\
Q &= \varepsilon_{\text{r}} Q_{0}, \\
U &= U_{0}, \\
W &= \varepsilon_{\text{r}} W_{0}.
\end{align*}

由功能关系，得
\begin{align*}
A_{2} + A_{\text{电源}} &= W - W_{0} = (\varepsilon_{\text{r}} - 1) W_{0}, \\
A_{\text{电源}} &= (Q - Q_{0}) U_{0} = (\varepsilon_{\text{r}} - 1) Q_{0} U_{0} = 2(\varepsilon_{\text{r}} - 1) W_{0}, \\
\Rightarrow A_{2} &= -(\varepsilon_{\text{r}} - 1) W_{0} < 0, \quad \text{$W_{0}$ 表达式见前。}
\end{align*}

$F$ 作负功，$F$ 方向朝左，实为拉力，避免介质块被电场力快速吸入。

raw_question_text: 如电图 2.5.1 所示，$C^+$和$C^-$是沿 $z$ 轴放置的两根互相绝缘的长直非磁性导体，其中的电流分别沿 $z$ 轴的正方向和负方向流动，电流强度均为 $I$。电图 2.5.1 中画斜线的部分是两导体的正截面，它们分别是 $xy$ 平面中的部分圆，两圆的直径均为 $D$，两圆心的间距为 $\frac{D}{2}$，不难算出，两导体正截面的面积均为 $\left(\frac{1}{12}\pi+\frac{1}{8}\sqrt{3}\right)D^{2}$。每一根导体中的电流均匀地分布在自身的正截面内。试求两根导体之间空白区域内的磁场分布 $B(x, y)$。
normalized_question_text: 如电图 2.5.1 所示,$C^+$和$C^-$是沿 $z$ 轴放置的两根互相绝缘的长直非磁性导体,其中的电流分别沿 $z$ 轴的正方向和负方向流动,电流强度均为 $I$。电图 2.5.1 中画斜线的部分是两导体的正截面,它们分别是 $xy$ 平面中的部分圆,两圆的直径均为 $D$,两圆心的间距为 $\frac{D}{2}$,不难算出,两导体正截面的面积均为 $\left(\frac{1}{12}\pi+\frac{1}{8}\sqrt{3}\right)D^{2}$。每一根导体中的电流均匀地分布在自身的正截面内。试求两根导体之间空白区域内的磁场分布 $B(x, y)$。
reasoning_chain:
【分析与解】空白区域内的磁场可以看作是两根完整的圆柱形长直载流导体产生的磁场之和，所谓完整的圆柱形是指其正截面是完整的圆而不是部分圆，由于两根完整的圆柱形长直导体中的电流反向，因此在交叠区域中两个反向电流刚好抵消，形成如本题所述的无电流空白区域。设两根完整圆柱形长直导体中的电流为$I'$，则$I'$与题目给定的$I$之比应为完整圆与部分圆的面积之比（均设电流在正截面内均匀分布），即有
$$
\frac{I}{I'} = \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{12} + \frac{\sqrt{3}}{8}\right)D^2}{\frac{\pi}{4}D^2} = \frac{2\pi + 3\sqrt{3}}{6\pi}
$$

由安培环路定理，在沿$z$轴放置的长直圆柱体中，若电流沿$z$轴正方向（电图2.5.1中垂直纸面向外）流动，电流强度为$I'$且均匀分布，则在距圆柱体中轴为$r$处的磁场$\boldsymbol{B}$只有角分量$B_\varphi$，其大小为
$$
B_\varphi = \frac{\mu_0}{2\pi r}\left(\frac{\pi r^2}{\frac{\pi}{4}D^2}I'\right) = \frac{2\mu_0 I' r}{\pi D^2}
$$
$B_\varphi$的$x$分量和$y$分量分别为
$$
B_x = B_\varphi \cdot \frac{-y}{r} = -\frac{2\mu_0 I'}{\pi D^2}y, \quad B_y = B_\varphi \cdot \frac{x}{r} = \frac{2\mu_0 I'}{\pi D^2}x
$$
这里的$x$和$y$坐标的原点取在圆柱体的中轴上。在本题中，如电图2.5.1所示，$x$、$y$坐标的原点取在空白区域的中心，即$y$坐标不变，$x$坐标与上述相比有所平移，故左侧完整长直圆柱体，其中电流沿$z$轴正方向，电流强度为$I'$，它在空白区域中任一点$(x,y)$产生的磁场的$x$分量和$y$分量分别为
$$
B_x^+ = -\frac{2\mu_0 I'}{\pi D^2}y, \quad B_y^+ = \frac{2\mu_0 I'}{\pi D^2}\left(x + \frac{D}{4}\right)
$$

同样，右侧完整长直圆柱体，其中电流沿$z$轴负方向，电流强度为$I'$，它在空白区域中任一点$(x,y)$产生的磁场的$x$分量和$y$分量分别为
$$
B_x^- = -\frac{2\mu_0 (-I')}{\pi D^2}y, \quad B_y^- = \frac{2\mu_0 (-I')}{\pi D^2}\left(x - \frac{D}{4}\right)
$$

本题的长直载流导体$C^+$和$C^-$在空白区域内任一点$(x,y)$产生的磁场的$x$分量和$y$分量，即为上述结果之和，即
$$
B_x = B_x^+ + B_x^- = 0, \quad B_y = B_y^+ + B_y^- = \frac{\mu_0 I'}{\pi D}
$$

把
$$
I' = \frac{6\pi}{2\pi + 3\sqrt{3}}I
$$
代入，得
$$
\begin{cases}
B_x = 0 \\
B_y = \frac{6\mu_0}{(2\pi + 3\sqrt{3})D}I
\end{cases}
$$
可见空白区域内的磁场为均匀磁场，其方向沿$y$轴正方向。

raw_question_text:
在坚直平面上设置 $O x y$ 坐标面，形如图示的星形线其参数方程为
$$
x=A \cos ^{3} \phi, y=A \sin ^{3} \phi .
$$
（注意，$\phi$ 并非 $O x y$ 坐标面上矢径的幅角）
沿参数 $\phi$ 从 $\pi$ 到 $\frac{3}{2} \pi$ 一段曲线设置一条无摩擦的轨道，令小球 $P$从 $A$ 无初速地沿轨道上侧下滑。 确定 $P$ 离开轨道的坐标值 $x_{0}$
normalized_question_text:
在坚直平面上设置 $Oxy$ 坐标面，形如图示的星形线其参数方程为
$$
x=A\cos^3\phi,\ y=A\sin^3\phi.
$$
（注意，$\phi$ 并非 $Oxy$ 坐标面上矢径的幅角。）
沿参数 $\phi$ 从 $\pi$ 到 $\frac{3}{2}\pi$ 的一段曲线设置一条无摩擦轨道，令小球 $P$ 从 $A$ 无初速地沿轨道上侧下滑。确定 $P$ 离开轨道时的坐标值 $x_0$。
reasoning_chain:
$P$ 下滑速度
$$
v=\sqrt{2 g(-y)}=\sqrt{-2 g A \sin ^{3} \phi}, \quad \Rightarrow \quad v^{2}=-2 g A \sin ^{3} \phi,
$$
$\phi$ 对应的向心加速度
$$
a_{\text{心}}=v^{2} / \rho=-2 g A \sin ^{3} \phi / 3 A \cos \phi \sin \phi=-\frac{2}{3} g \frac{\sin ^{2} \phi}{\cos \phi},
$$

设 $\phi_{0}$ 处滑离轨道，则应有
$$
\begin{array}{c}
a_{\text{心}}=g \cos \alpha=g \frac{-v_{y}}{v}=-\left.g \cos \phi\right|_{\phi=\phi_{0}}, 
a_{\text{心}}=-\left.\frac{2}{3} g \frac{\sin ^{2} \phi}{\cos \phi}\right|_{\phi=\phi_{0}},
\end{array}
$$

得
$$
\begin{array}{c}
\tan \phi_{0}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}, \quad\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\sin \phi_{0}=-\sqrt{3 / 5}, 
\cos \phi_{0}=-\sqrt{2 / 5},
\end{array} \quad\left(\phi_{0}: \text { III象限 }\right)\right. 
x_{0}=A \cos ^{3} \phi_{0}=-\frac{2}{5} \sqrt{\frac{2}{5}} A=-0.253 A . 
\left(y_{0}=A \sin ^{3} \phi_{0}=-\frac{3}{5} \sqrt{\frac{3}{5}} A=-0.465 A\right)
\end{array}
$$

raw_question_text: 在坚直平面上设置图示的水平 $x$ 轴和坚直向下的 $y$ 轴，$t=0$时刻位于 $x=0, y=0$ 处的小水桶从静止出发，以匀加速度 $a_{0}$ 沿 $x$轴运动。过程中桶底小孔向下漏水，单位时间漏水质量为常量 $m_{0}$ 。略去漏水相对水桶的初速度，在任意 $t_{0}>0$ 时刻，试求漏水迹线中的质量线密度 $\lambda$ 随 $y$ 坐标的分布函数，
normalized_question_text: 在坚直平面上设置图示的水平 $x$ 轴和坚直向下的 $y$ 轴，$t=0$时刻位于 $x=0, y=0$ 处的小水桶从静止出发，以匀加速度 $a_{0}$ 沿 $x$ 轴运动。过程中桶底小孔向下漏水，单位时间漏水质量为常量 $m_{0}$。略去漏水相对水桶的初速度，在任意 $t_{0}>0$ 时刻，试求漏水迹线中的质量线密度 $\lambda$ 随 $y$ 坐标的分布函数。
reasoning_chain:
如前所述，$t_{0}$ 之前，于 $t$ 时刻从桶底漏出的水在 $t_{0}$ 时刻的 $x, y$ 坐标分别为
$$
\begin{array}{c}
x(t)=\frac{1}{2} a_{0} t_{0}^{2}-\frac{1}{2} a_{0}\left(t_{0}-t\right)^{2}, 
y(t)=\frac{1}{2} g\left(t_{0}-t\right)^{2} .
\end{array}
$$
$t_{0}$ 之前，于 $t+\mathrm{d} t$ 时刻从桶底漏出的水在 $t_{0}$ 时刻的 $x, y$ 坐标分别为
$$
x(t+\mathrm{d} t), y(t+\mathrm{d} t) .
$$
$\mathrm{d} t$ 时间内质量为 $\mathrm{d} m=m_{0} \mathrm{~d} t$ 的漏水，在 $t_{0}$ 时刻迹线中占据的 $\mathrm{d} x, \mathrm{~d} y$ 以及长度 $\mathrm{d} l$ 为
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\mathrm{d} x=a_{0}\left(t_{0}-t\right) \mathrm{d} t, \quad \mathrm{~d} y=-g\left(t_{0}-t\right) \mathrm{d} t, 
\mathrm{~d} l=\sqrt{\mathrm{d} x^{2}+\mathrm{d} y^{2}}=\sqrt{a_{0}^{2}+g^{2}}\left(t_{0}-t\right) \mathrm{d} t,
\end{array}
$$

其中 $\mathrm{d} y$ 为负，是因为 $y(t)>y(t+\mathrm{d} t)$ ，如题解图 3 所示。迹线中 $y(t)$ 处质量线密度为
$$
\lambda(t)=\frac{\mathrm{d} m}{\mathrm{~d} l}=m_{0} / \sqrt{a_{0}^{2}+g^{2}}\left(t_{0}-t\right),
$$

将
$$
t_{0}-t=\sqrt{2 y / g}
$$

代入，即得 $t_{0}$ 时刻迹线中质量线密度 $\lambda$ 随 $y$ 坐标的分布式：
$$
\lambda(y)=\frac{m_{0}}{\sqrt{a_{0}^{2}+g^{2}}} \sqrt{\frac{g}{2 y}} .
$$

raw_question_text: A plane wave with free-space wavelength $\lambda_{0}$ is normally incident on a plane-parallel glass plate of stepped thickness $d_{1}$ and $d_{2}$ and refractive index $n$. The plate is submerged in a liquid with refractive index $n_{0}$. Determine the phase difference $\Delta \phi_{12}$ between the wave oscillations at points $\mathrm{P}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{P}_{2}$, lying in the plane of the back surface of the plate with greater thickness $d_{1}$.
normalized_question_text: A plane wave with free-space wavelength $\lambda_{0}$ is normally incident on a plane-parallel glass plate of stepped thickness $d_{1}$ and $d_{2}$ and refractive index $n$. The plate is submerged in a liquid with refractive index $n_{0}$.
